```{r Petal plot of weighted means per axis scaled 0 to 1}
    spatial_scale = c("Local","National","Regional","Global","None")
  )

factor_level_lookup <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(factor_levels), function(fn) {
    tibble(
      factor_name  = fn,
      factor_level = factor_levels[[fn]],
      factor_num   = seq_along(factor_levels[[fn]])
    )
  })
)


petal_df <- join_oro_with_multilabels(
  oro_df = oro_df,
  codedVars = codedVars,
  label_cols = c("governance_body", "rules_of_law", "spatial_scale", "time_scale"),
  label_levels_list = factor_levels,
  id_col = "id",
  make_summary = FALSE
) %>%
  filter(!if_any(everything(), ~ grepl("None", .x))) %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      where(is.factor),
      ~droplevels(.x)
    ) 
  )%>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(axisCols),   # melt only axis columns
    names_to = "factor_name",  # column names become factor_name
    values_to = "factor_level" # factor values become factor_level
  )%>%
  left_join(
    factor_level_lookup, by = c("factor_name","factor_level")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    weighted_axis = weight*factor_num
  ) %>%
  group_by(
    id, oro_type, factor_name
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    weighted_axis = sum(weighted_axis, na.rm=T)
  )%>%
  group_by(oro_type, factor_name)%>%
  summarise(
    weighted_axis = mean(weighted_axis, na.rm=T)
  )%>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = factor_name,
    values_from = weighted_axis
  ) %>%
  
  # Join with outcomes data
  left_join(propPosOutcomes, by = c("oro_type")) %>%
  select(-n_positive, -total) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      where(is.numeric),
      ~scales::rescale(.x) # rescale_signed(.x)
    )
  )%>%
  ungroup()


# Format for ggplot
allAxisCols <- axisCols
allAxisColsLabs <- c("Governance\nbody\nscale","Law","Spatial\nscale","Time\nscale","% Adaptive")

ymn <- floor(min(unlist(c(petal_df[,allAxisCols])), na.rm=T))
ymx <- ceiling(max(unlist(c(petal_df[,allAxisCols])), na.rm=T))
grid_lines <- pretty(c(ymn, ymx))


petal_df <- petal_df %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("governance_body", "rules_of_law", "spatial_scale", "time_scale","prop_positive"), names_to = "category", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    category = factor(
      category, 
      levels = c("governance_body", "rules_of_law", "spatial_scale", "time_scale","prop_positive"),
      labels = allAxisColsLabs
    )
  )

# Label positions: use max value per category so labels are outside all petals
label_df <- petal_df %>%
  group_by(category) %>%
  summarise(value = max(value)) %>%
  mutate(id = as.numeric(factor(category))) %>%
  mutate(angle = 90 - 360 * (id - 0.5) / n() + 90) %>%
  mutate(angle = ifelse(angle > 90 & angle < 270 | angle < -90, angle + 180, angle),
           hjust = 0.5, 
         )


petal_ggp <- ggplot(petal_df, aes(x = category, y = value, fill = category)) +
  facet_wrap(vars(oro_type), labeller = label_wrap_gen(width=30))+
  geom_col(width = 1, color = "white") +
  # # labels
  # geom_text(data = label_df,
  #           aes(x = category, y = value + 0.5, 
  #               label = category, angle = angle, hjust=hjust),
  #           inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 2) +
  coord_polar(clip = "off") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    # limits = c(ymn, ymx),
    breaks = grid_lines
  )+
  geom_text(
    data = data.frame(
      x=rep(0, length(grid_lines)-1),
      y=grid_lines[2:(length(grid_lines))]
    ),
    aes(x=x, y=y, label=y),
    size=2,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  )+
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2")+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(margin = margin(t=20), vjust=0),
    strip.text = element_text(size=9),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    # panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "lightgrey", linewidth = 0.5),
  )

petal_ggp


## Save plot

ggsave(
  here::here("figures/supplemental/petal_plot_oro_noEvidenceType.png"),
  petal_ggp,
  width = 6, height = 7.5, dpi=600
)

ggsave(
  here::here("figures/supplemental/petal_plot_oro_noEvidenceType.pdf"),
  petal_ggp,
  width = 6, height = 7.5
)
```


```{r Petal plot of weighted means per axis ranked and excl adaptive axis}


## Get axes from coded variables
axisCols <- c("governance_body", "rules_of_law", "spatial_scale", "time_scale")

factor_levels = list(
    # evidence_type = c("Quantitative metric","Social empirical","Qualitative - other"),
    governance_body = c("Community-based","Private organizations/companies",
                        "National government",
                        "Regional (e.g. RFMOs)","International"
                        ),
    rules_of_law = c("Law (binding)","Non-binding"),
    time_scale = c("<= 1 month","1 month <= 1 year","1 year <= 5 years","5 years <= 10 years","> 10 years","None"),
    spatial_scale = c("Local","National","Regional","Global","None")
  )

factor_level_lookup <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(factor_levels), function(fn) {
    tibble(
      factor_name  = fn,
      factor_level = factor_levels[[fn]],
      factor_num   = seq_along(factor_levels[[fn]])
    )
  })
)


petal_df <- join_oro_with_multilabels(
  oro_df = oro_df,
  codedVars = codedVars,
  label_cols = c("governance_body", "rules_of_law", "spatial_scale", "time_scale"),
  label_levels_list = factor_levels,
  id_col = "id",
  make_summary = FALSE
) %>%
  filter(!if_any(everything(), ~ grepl("None", .x))) %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      where(is.factor),
      ~droplevels(.x)
    ) 
  )%>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(axisCols),   # melt only axis columns
    names_to = "factor_name",  # column names become factor_name
    values_to = "factor_level" # factor values become factor_level
  )%>%
  left_join(
    factor_level_lookup, by = c("factor_name","factor_level")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    weighted_axis = weight*factor_num
  ) %>%
  group_by(
    id, oro_type, factor_name
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    weighted_axis = sum(weighted_axis, na.rm=T)
  )%>%
  group_by(oro_type, factor_name)%>%
  summarise(
    weighted_axis = mean(weighted_axis, na.rm=T)
  )%>%
  # Calculate ranking
  arrange(factor_name, weighted_axis)%>%
  group_by(factor_name)%>%
  mutate(
    axis_rank = row_number()
  )%>%
  # pivot
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = factor_name,
    values_from = axis_rank
  ) %>%
  ungroup() 
  



# Format for ggplot
allAxisCols <- axisCols
allAxisColsLabs <- c("Governance\nbody scale","Law","Spatial\nscale","Time\nscale")

ymn <- floor(min(unlist(c(petal_df[,allAxisCols])), na.rm=T))
ymx <- ceiling(max(unlist(c(petal_df[,allAxisCols])), na.rm=T))
grid_lines <- pretty(c(ymn, ymx),n = 3)


petal_df <- petal_df %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("governance_body", "rules_of_law", "spatial_scale", "time_scale"), names_to = "category", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    category = factor(
      category, 
      levels = c("governance_body", "rules_of_law", "spatial_scale", "time_scale"),
      labels = allAxisColsLabs
    ),
    id = as.numeric(factor(category))
  )

# Label positions: use max value per category so labels are outside all petals
label_df <- petal_df %>%
  group_by(category) %>%
  summarise(value = max(value, na.rm=T)) %>%
  mutate(id = as.numeric(factor(category))) %>%
  mutate(angle = 360 * (id - 0.5) / n() + 360) %>%
  mutate(angle = ifelse(angle > 90 & angle < 270 | angle < -90, angle + 180, angle),
           hjust = 0.5, 
         )
label_df <- petal_df %>%
  group_by(category) %>%
  summarise(value = max(value, na.rm=T)) %>%
  mutate(
    id = as.numeric(factor(category, levels = levels(petal_df$category))),
    # midpoint of each bar in radial coordinates
    angle_raw = (360 * (id - 0.5) / nlevels(petal_df$category))-135,
    # flip text that would be upside-down
    angle = ifelse(angle_raw > 90 & angle_raw < 270,
                   angle_raw + 180,
                   angle_raw),
    hjust = ifelse(angle_raw > 90 & angle_raw < 270, 1, 0)
  ) 

label_df


petal_ggp <- ggplot(petal_df, aes(x = category, y = value, fill = category)) +
  facet_wrap(vars(oro_type), labeller = label_wrap_gen(width=30))+
  geom_col(width = 1, color = "white", position = position_identity()) +
  coord_radial(theta = "x", start = 0, thetalim = c(0,4), clip="off", expand = FALSE)+
  # labels
  geom_text(data = label_df,
            aes(x = category, y = value + 1,
                label = category, angle = angle),
            hjust = 0.5, vjust=0, inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 2.5) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = grid_lines,
    expand = expansion(c(0,0.1))
  )+
  geom_text(
    data = data.frame(
      x=rep(0, length(grid_lines)-1),
      y=grid_lines[2:(length(grid_lines))]
    ),
    aes(x=x, y=y, label=y),
    size=2,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  )+
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2")+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), #element_text(margin = margin(t=-5), vjust=0, hjust=1),
    strip.text = element_text(size=10),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
   
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "lightgrey", linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),

    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
 
    panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines")
  )

# petal_ggp


ggsave(
  here::here("figures/main/petal_plot_oro_ranking.pdf"),
  petal_ggp,
  width = 6, height = 7.5
)
```




```{r Bar plot version of Petal plot - of weighted means per axis ranked and excl adaptive axis}


## Get axes from coded variables
axisCols <- c("governance_body", "rules_of_law", "spatial_scale", "time_scale")

factor_levels = list(
    # evidence_type = c("Quantitative metric","Social empirical","Qualitative - other"),
    governance_body = c("Community-based","Private organizations/companies",
                        "National government",
                        "Regional (e.g. RFMOs)","International"
                        ),
    rules_of_law = c("Law (binding)","Non-binding"),
    time_scale = c("<= 1 month","1 month <= 1 year","1 year <= 5 years","5 years <= 10 years","> 10 years","None"),
    spatial_scale = c("Local","National","Regional","Global","None")
  )

factor_level_lookup <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(factor_levels), function(fn) {
    tibble(
      factor_name  = fn,
      factor_level = factor_levels[[fn]],
      factor_num   = seq_along(factor_levels[[fn]])
    )
  })
)


petal_df <- join_oro_with_multilabels(
  oro_df = oro_df,
  codedVars = codedVars,
  label_cols = c("governance_body", "rules_of_law", "spatial_scale", "time_scale"),
  label_levels_list = factor_levels,
  id_col = "id",
  make_summary = FALSE
) %>%
  filter(!if_any(everything(), ~ grepl("None", .x))) %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      where(is.factor),
      ~droplevels(.x)
    ) 
  )%>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(axisCols),   # melt only axis columns
    names_to = "factor_name",  # column names become factor_name
    values_to = "factor_level" # factor values become factor_level
  )%>%
  left_join(
    factor_level_lookup, by = c("factor_name","factor_level")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    weighted_axis = weight*factor_num
  ) %>%
  group_by(
    id, oro_type, factor_name
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    weighted_axis = sum(weighted_axis, na.rm=T)
  )%>%
  group_by(oro_type, factor_name)%>%
  summarise(
    weighted_axis = mean(weighted_axis, na.rm=T)
  )%>%
  # Calculate ranking
  arrange(factor_name, weighted_axis)%>%
  group_by(factor_name)%>%
  mutate(
    axis_rank = row_number()
  )%>%
  # pivot
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = factor_name,
    values_from = axis_rank
  ) %>%
  ungroup() 
  



# Format for ggplot
allAxisCols <- axisCols
allAxisColsLabs <- c("Scale of governing body","Rule of law","Spatial scale of implementation","Time since implementation")

ymn <- floor(min(unlist(c(petal_df[,allAxisCols])), na.rm=T))
ymx <- ceiling(max(unlist(c(petal_df[,allAxisCols])), na.rm=T))
grid_lines <- pretty(c(ymn, ymx),n = 3)


petal_df <- petal_df %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("governance_body", "rules_of_law", "spatial_scale", "time_scale"), names_to = "category", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    category = factor(
      category, 
      levels = c("governance_body", "rules_of_law", "spatial_scale", "time_scale"),
      labels = allAxisColsLabs
    ),
    id = as.numeric(factor(category))
  )

# Label positions: use max value per category so labels are outside all petals
label_df <- petal_df %>%
  group_by(category) %>%
  summarise(value = max(value, na.rm=T)) %>%
  mutate(id = as.numeric(factor(category))) %>%
  mutate(angle = 360 * (id - 0.5) / n() + 360) %>%
  mutate(angle = ifelse(angle > 90 & angle < 270 | angle < -90, angle + 180, angle),
           hjust = 0.5, 
         )
label_df <- petal_df %>%
  group_by(category) %>%
  summarise(value = max(value, na.rm=T)) %>%
  mutate(
    id = as.numeric(factor(category, levels = levels(petal_df$category))),
    # midpoint of each bar in radial coordinates
    angle_raw = (360 * (id - 0.5) / nlevels(petal_df$category))-135,
    # flip text that would be upside-down
    angle = ifelse(angle_raw > 90 & angle_raw < 270,
                   angle_raw + 180,
                   angle_raw),
    hjust = ifelse(angle_raw > 90 & angle_raw < 270, 1, 0)
  ) 

label_df


oro_context_bar_ggp <- ggplot(petal_df, aes(x = oro_type, y = value, fill = oro_type)) +
  facet_wrap(vars(category), labeller = label_wrap_gen(width=30))+
  geom_col(width = 1, color = "white", position = position_identity()) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "Ranking",
    breaks = grid_lines,
    expand = expansion(c(0,0.1))
  )+
  scale_fill_manual(breaks = oroTypeAes$levels, 
                    values = oroTypeAes$col)+
  coord_cartesian(clip="off")+
  labs(
    x = "Option type"
  )+
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)+
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust=1, hjust=1),
    strip.text = element_text(size=10),
   
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "lightgrey", linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),

    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
 
    panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"),
    plot.margin = unit(c(0.5, 0.5, 1,1), "cm")
  )

oro_context_bar_ggp


ggsave(
  here::here("figures/main/bar_plot_oro_ranking.pdf"),
  oro_context_bar_ggp,
  width = 6, height = 6
)


## ANd try writing bar plots seperately and writing as svg
categories <- levels(petal_df$category)
oro_context_bar_ggp_list <- lapply(
  1:length(categories),
  function(i){
    cat <- categories[i]
    tmp_dat <- petal_df %>% filter(category == cat)
    tmp_plot <- ggplot(tmp_dat, aes(x = oro_type, y = value, fill = oro_type)) +
      geom_col(width = 1, color = "white", position = position_identity()) +
      scale_y_continuous(
        name = "Ranking",
        breaks = grid_lines,
        expand = expansion(c(0,0.1))
      )+
      scale_fill_manual(breaks = oroTypeAes$levels, 
                        values = oroTypeAes$col)+
      guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow=4, title = ""))+
      coord_cartesian(clip="off")+
      labs(
        title = paste(letters[i]),
        subtitle = stringr::str_wrap(cat, width = 30),
        x = ""
      )+
      theme_minimal(base_size = 11)+
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(face="bold", size=15),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust=1, hjust=1),
        strip.text = element_text(size=10),
       
        panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "lightgrey", linewidth = 0.5),
        panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
    
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
     
        panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"),
        plot.margin = unit(c(0.5, 0.5, 1,1), "cm")
      )
    
    if(i==1){
      tmp_leg <- ggpubr::get_legend(tmp_plot)
      ggsave(
        filename = here::here(
          "figures/main/bar_plot_oro_ranking_svg_plots/oro_fill_legend.svg"
        ),
        plot = tmp_leg,
        width=4, height=1.5
      )
    }
    
    
    ggsave(
      filename = here::here(paste0(
        "figures/main/bar_plot_oro_ranking_svg_plots/panel_",letters[i],".svg"
      )),
      plot = tmp_plot+theme(legend.position = "none"),
      width=4, height=4
    )
  }
  
)

```


```{r oro and dimension grouping of proportion of articles an oro is involved in in that factor that have each level}


## Get axes from coded variables
axisCols <- c("governance_body", "rules_of_law", "spatial_scale", "time_scale")
allAxisColsLabs <- c("Scale of governing body","Rule of law","Spatial scale of implementation","Time since implementation")


factor_levels = list(
    # evidence_type = c("Quantitative metric","Social empirical","Qualitative - other"),
    governance_body = c("Community-based","Private organizations/companies",
                        "National government",
                        "Regional (e.g. RFMOs)","International"
                        ),
    rules_of_law = c("Non-binding","Law (binding)"),
    time_scale = c("<= 1 month","1 month <= 1 year","1 year <= 5 years","5 years <= 10 years","> 10 years","None"),
    spatial_scale = c("Local","National","Regional","Global","None")
  )

factor_level_lookup <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(factor_levels), function(fn) {
    tibble(
      factor_name  = fn,
      factor_level = factor_levels[[fn]],
      factor_num   = seq_along(factor_levels[[fn]])
    )
  })
)

dashboard_df <- join_oro_with_multilabels(
  oro_df = oro_df,
  codedVars = codedVars,
  label_cols = c("governance_body", "rules_of_law", "spatial_scale", "time_scale"),
  label_levels_list = factor_levels,
  id_col = "id",
  make_summary = FALSE
) %>%
  filter(!if_any(everything(), ~ grepl("None", .x))) %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      where(is.factor),
      ~droplevels(.x)
    ) 
  )%>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(axisCols),   # melt only axis columns
    names_to = "factor_name",  # column names become factor_name
    values_to = "factor_level" # factor values become factor_level
  )%>%
  left_join(
    factor_level_lookup, by = c("factor_name","factor_level")
  ) %>%
  group_by(
    oro_type, factor_name, factor_level
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n_distinct(id),
    n_weighted = sum(weight, na.rm=T),
    factor_num = mean(factor_num, na.rm=T)
  ) %>%
  arrange(factor_name, factor_level, n)%>%
  ungroup() 

dash_oro_totals <- dashboard_df %>%
  group_by(oro_type, factor_name) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()

dashboard_df <- dashboard_df %>%
  left_join(dash_oro_totals)%>%
  mutate(
    prop = n/total,
    prop_weighted = prop*factor_num
  ) %>%
  # Calculate ranking
  group_by(
    oro_type, factor_name
  )%>%
  summarise(
    prop_weighted_factor_sum = sum(prop_weighted, na.rm=T),
    n = sum(n, na.rm = T),
    n_weighted = sum(n_weighted, na.rm=T)
  ) %>%
  arrange(factor_name, prop_weighted_factor_sum) %>%
  group_by(factor_name)%>%
  mutate(
    axis_rank = row_number(),
    category = factor(
      factor_name,
      levels = axisCols,
      labels = allAxisColsLabs
    )
  )

## chi-squared tests -------------------
dash_model_df <- dashboard_df %>%
  select(factor_name, oro_type, axis_rank) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = c("factor_name"), values_from = axis_rank) %>%
  arrange(oro_type)

gov_cor_test <- cor.test(
  dash_model_df$governance_body,
  dash_model_df$rules_of_law,
  method = "kendall",
  exact = TRUE
)
gov_cor_test
# T = 13, p-value = 0.9049
# alternative hypothesis: true tau is not equal to 0
# sample estimates:
#         tau 
# -0.07142857 

scale_cor_test <- cor.test(
  dash_model_df$spatial_scale,
  dash_model_df$time_scale,
  method = "kendall",
  exact = TRUE
)
scale_cor_test
# T = 17, p-value = 0.5484
# alternative hypothesis: true tau is not equal to 0
# sample estimates:
#       tau 
# 0.2142857  


## bar plot facetted by dimension -------------
oro_context_bar_ggp <- ggplot(dashboard_df, aes(x = oro_type, y = axis_rank, fill = oro_type)) +
  facet_wrap(vars(category), labeller = label_wrap_gen(width=30))+
  geom_col(width = 1, color = "white", position = position_identity()) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "Ranking",
    breaks = grid_lines,
    expand = expansion(c(0,0.1))
  )+
  scale_fill_manual(breaks = oroTypeAes$levels, 
                    values = oroTypeAes$col)+
  coord_cartesian(clip="off")+
  labs(
    x = "Option type"
  )+
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)+
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust=1, hjust=1),
    strip.text = element_text(size=10),
   
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "lightgrey", linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),

    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
 
    panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"),
    plot.margin = unit(c(0.5, 0.5, 1,1), "cm")
  )

oro_context_bar_ggp

# Save
ggsave(
  here::here("figures/main/bar_plot_dimension_ranking.pdf"),
  oro_context_bar_ggp,
  width = 6, height = 6
)



## bar plot facetted by oro ------------
oro_context_bar_switch_ggp <- ggplot(dashboard_df, aes(x = category, y = axis_rank, fill = category)) +
  facet_wrap(vars(oro_type), labeller = label_wrap_gen(width=30),
             ncol=2)+
  geom_col(width = 1, color = "white", position = position_identity()) +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::number(n_weighted, accuracy = 0.1)), vjust=0, size=3)+
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "Ranking",
    breaks = grid_lines,
    labels = c("low", rep("", length(grid_lines)-2), 'high'),
    expand = expansion(c(0,0.1))
  )+
  scale_x_discrete(
    limits = levels(dashboard_df$category),
    labels = c("Governance","Law","Spatial scale","Time scale")
  )+
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2")+
  coord_cartesian(clip="off")+
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(nrow=2)
  )+
  labs(
    x = "Criterion",
    fill = ""
  )+
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)+
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust=1, hjust=1),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r=10)),
    strip.text = element_text(size=10),
   
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "lightgrey", linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),

    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
 
    panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"),
    plot.margin = unit(c(0.5, 0.5, 1,1), "cm"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12)
  )

oro_context_bar_switch_ggp

# Save
ggsave(
  here::here("figures/main/bar_plot_oro_ranking.pdf"),
  oro_context_bar_switch_ggp,
  width = 5, height = 7
)



## And quick as a petal plot -----------
petal_ggp <- ggplot(dashboard_df, aes(x = category, y = axis_rank, fill = category)) +
  facet_wrap(vars(oro_type), labeller = label_wrap_gen(width=20))+
  geom_col(width = 1, color = "white", position = position_identity()) +
  coord_radial(theta = "x", start = 0, thetalim = c(0,4), clip="off", expand = FALSE)+
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = grid_lines,
    expand = expansion(c(0,0.1))
  )+
  scale_x_discrete(
    limits = levels(dashboard_df$category),
    labels = c("Governance","Law","Spatial","Time")
  )+
  geom_text(
    data = data.frame(
      x=rep(0, length(grid_lines)-1),
      y=grid_lines[2:(length(grid_lines))]
    ),
    aes(x=x, y=y, label=y),
    size=2,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  )+
  guides(
    theta = guide_axis_theta(angle = 0),
    fill = guide_legend(nrow=2)
  )+
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2")+
  labs(
    fill = "Dimension"
  )+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    # axis.text.x = element_text(margin = margin(t=-5), vjust=0, hjust=1),
    strip.text = element_text(size=10, margin = margin(b=8)),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "lightgrey", linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),

    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
 
    panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines")
  )

petal_ggp


ggsave(
  here::here("figures/main/petal_plot_oro_ranking.pdf"),
  petal_ggp,
  width = 6, height = 7.5
)



## Write dashboard plots for each dimension category seperately and save as svg -----------
categories <- levels(dashboard_df$category)
plot_icons <- c(
  "governance_icon.svg","law_icon.svg","spatial_icon.svg","time_icon.svg"
)
plot_icons <- here::here("figures/ranking icons inkscape", plot_icons)

oro_context_dashboard_ggp_list <- list()

for(i in 1:length(categories)){
    cat <- categories[i]
    icon_use <- magick::image_read_svg(plot_icons[i])
    
    tmp_dat <- dashboard_df %>% filter(category == cat)
    tmp_plot <- ggplot(tmp_dat, aes(x = oro_type, y = axis_rank, fill = oro_type)) +
      geom_col(width = 1, color = "white", position = position_identity()) +
      scale_y_continuous(
        name = "",
        breaks = c(0,8),
        labels = c("low", "high"),
        expand = expansion(c(0,0.1))
      )+
      # x axis order in terms of rank not type of ORO?
      scale_x_discrete(labels = label_wrap_gen(width=15)
                       # limits = tmp_dat$oro_type[order(tmp_dat$axis_rank,decreasing = TRUE)]
                       )+
      scale_fill_manual(breaks = oroTypeAes$levels, 
                        values = oroTypeAes$col)+
      guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow=4, title = ""),
             theta = guide_axis_theta(angle = 90),
             r     = guide_axis(angle = 0))+
      coord_radial(end = -0.5 * pi, start = 0.5 * pi,
                   inner.radius = 0.7, r.axis.inside = TRUE)+
      labs(
        title = paste(letters[i]),
        subtitle = stringr::str_wrap(cat, width = 30),
        x = ""
      )+
      theme_minimal(base_size = 11)+
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(face="bold", size=15, margin = margin(b=15, unit="pt")),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size=12, margin = margin(b=40, unit="pt")),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, vjust=1, hjust=1),
        strip.text = element_text(size=10),
        axis.line.theta = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.8),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),# element_line(colour = "lightgrey", linewidth = 0.5),
        panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
    
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
     
        panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"),
        plot.margin = unit(c(0.5, 1, 1,1), "cm"),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12)
      )
    # tmp_plot 
      
    
    tmp_plot_icon <- cowplot::ggdraw(tmp_plot+theme(legend.position = "none"))+ 
      cowplot::draw_image(icon_use, x = 0.52, y=0.45, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0,width = 0.28, height = 0.28, scale=1, halign = 0.5, valign = 0.5)
    # tmp_plot_icon
    
    ggsave(
      filename = here::here(paste0(
        "figures/main/dashboard_plot_oro_ranking_svg_plots/panel_",letters[i],".svg"
      )),
      plot = tmp_plot_icon, #tmp_plot+theme(legend.position = "none"),
      width=4, height=4
    )
    
    if(i==1){
      tmp_leg <- ggpubr::get_legend(tmp_plot)
      ggsave(
        filename = here::here(
          "figures/main/dashboard_plot_oro_ranking_svg_plots/oro_fill_legend.svg"
        ),
        plot = tmp_leg,
        width=4, height=1.5
      )
    }
    
    oro_context_dashboard_ggp_list[[i]] <- tmp_plot_icon
    
}
  


## plot all panels together
require(patchwork)
plot_layouts <- "
AB#
ABF
CDF
EEF"

all_plots_list <- c(oro_context_dashboard_ggp_list,
                    list(tmp_leg), 
                    list(oro_context_bar_switch_ggp+ggtitle("e.")+
                           theme(
                             plot.title = element_text(face="bold")
                             # plot.margin = unit(c(80,5,5,10),"pt")
                           )
                         ))

patch_plots <- wrap_plots(all_plots_list, design = plot_layouts, 
                          widths = c(1,1,1.3), heights = c(0.1,0.9,1,0.05))

ggsave(
  here::here("figures/main/dashboard_and_barplots_rankings.pdf"),
  plot=patch_plots,
  width=13, height=10
)

```


```{r per-level heatmap of proportion of articles an oro is involved in in that factor that have that particular level}


## Get axes from coded variables
axisCols <- c("governance_body", "rules_of_law", "spatial_scale", "time_scale")
allAxisColsLabs <- c("Scale of governing body","Rule of law","Spatial scale of implementation","Time since implementation")


factor_levels = list(
    # evidence_type = c("Quantitative metric","Social empirical","Qualitative - other"),
    governance_body = c("Community-based","Private organizations/companies",
                        "National government",
                        "Regional (e.g. RFMOs)","International"
                        ),
    rules_of_law = c("Non-binding","Law (binding)"),
    time_scale = c("<= 1 month","1 month <= 1 year","1 year <= 5 years","5 years <= 10 years","> 10 years","None"),
    spatial_scale = c("Local","National","Regional","Global","None")
  )

factor_level_lookup <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(factor_levels), function(fn) {
    tibble(
      factor_name  = fn,
      factor_level = factor_levels[[fn]],
      factor_num   = seq_along(factor_levels[[fn]])
    )
  })
) 

factor_level_order <- factor_level_lookup %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    factor_name_f = factor(factor_name, levels = axisCols, labels = allAxisColsLabs),
  )%>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(factor_name_f, factor_num) %>%
  mutate(
    plot_order = row_number()
  )



# Instead of taking mean weighted averages, 
# calculate rankings from the proportion of articles an oro is involved in in that factor, that have that particular level.

oro_rank_df <- join_oro_with_multilabels(
  oro_df = oro_df,
  codedVars = codedVars,
  label_cols = c("governance_body", "rules_of_law", "spatial_scale", "time_scale"),
  label_levels_list = factor_levels,
  id_col = "id",
  make_summary = FALSE
) %>%
  filter(!if_any(everything(), ~ grepl("None", .x))) %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      where(is.factor),
      ~droplevels(.x)
    ) 
  )%>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(axisCols),   # melt only axis columns
    names_to = "factor_name",  # column names become factor_name
    values_to = "factor_level" # factor values become factor_level
  )%>%
  left_join(
    factor_level_lookup, by = c("factor_name","factor_level")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    weighted_axis = weight #*factor_num
  ) %>%
  group_by(
    oro_type, factor_name, factor_level
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n_distinct(id),
    n_weighted = sum(weight, na.rm=T)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

oro_totals <- oro_rank_df %>%
  group_by(oro_type, factor_name) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()

oro_rank_df <- oro_rank_df %>%
  left_join(oro_totals)%>%
  mutate(
    prop = n/total
  ) %>%
  # Calculate ranking
  arrange(factor_name, factor_level, prop) %>%
  group_by(factor_name, factor_level)%>%
  mutate(
    axis_rank = row_number()
  )%>%
  left_join(
    factor_level_order %>%
      select(factor_name_f, factor_name, factor_level, plot_order)
  )  


level_rank_mat_ggp <- ggplot(oro_rank_df,
                             aes(y=factor(plot_order), x=oro_type, fill=axis_rank))+
  facet_grid(rows = vars(factor_name_f), drop = TRUE, scales="free_y",
             labeller = label_wrap_gen(width = 10), switch = "y",
             space= "free_y"
             )+
  scale_y_discrete(
    breaks = factor_level_order$plot_order[order(factor_level_order$plot_order)],
    labels = factor_level_order$factor_level[order(factor_level_order$plot_order)],
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1)),
    drop=TRUE
  )+
  scale_x_discrete(limits = rev(levels(oro_rank_df$oro_type)), drop=TRUE)+
  geom_tile(height = 0.8)+
  geom_text(aes(label=scales::number(n_weighted, accuracy=0.1)), 
            color="red", size=3)+
  scale_fill_stepsn(
    breaks = function(in_limits){seq(in_limits[1], in_limits[2], by=1)},
    colors = viridis(8),
    labels = c("low", rep("", 6), "high")
  )+
  labs(
    x = "Option type",
    y="",
    fill = "Rank"
  )+
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)+
  theme(
    strip.placement = "outside",  
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle=45, hjust=1, vjust=1
    ),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    legend.key.width = unit(0.9, "cm"),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill=NA, color="black",linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    # axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )



ggsave(
  here::here("figures/main/oro_ranking_matrix.pdf"),
  level_rank_mat_ggp,
  width = 5.5, height = 7.5
)
```



```{r outcome type ~ qualitative outcome}
oro_outcomes <- expand_labels_with_weight(studyResults, article_outcome_id, oro_type) %>%
  rename(oro_type = label, oro_weight = weight)%>%
  mutate(
    oro_type = gsub("Socio behavioural", "Socio-behavioural", oro_type)
  )%>%
  mutate(
    oro_type = factor(
      oro_type,
      levels = oroTypeAes$levels
    )
  )


outcomes <- expand_labels_with_weight(studyResults, article_outcome_id, result_type) %>%
  rename(outcome_type = label, outcome_type_weight = weight) %>%
  left_join(oro_outcomes, by = "article_outcome_id", relationship = "many-to-many") %>%
  left_join(studyResults %>% select(id, article_outcome_id, data_type, result_effect), by = "article_outcome_id")
  



type_outcome_joined <- outcomes %>% #merge(oro_df, outcomes, by = "id", all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(weight = oro_weight*outcome_type_weight) %>%
  group_by(oro_type, outcome_type, data_type, result_effect) %>%
  summarise(
    weightedSum = sum(weight),
    oro_weight = sum(oro_weight)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  replace_na(list(outcome_type = "None", data_type = "None", result_effect = "None")) %>%
  mutate(
    weightedSum = ifelse(is.na(weightedSum), oro_weight, weightedSum),
    oro_type = factor(
      oro_type,
      levels = oroTypeAes$levels
    ),
    outcome_type = factor(
      outcome_type,
      levels = c("Economic","Social","Ecological","Management","None"), # CHANGE THIS
      labels = c("Economic","Social","Ecological","Management","Outcome\nnot identified")
    ),
    data_type = factor(
      data_type,
      levels = c("Quantitative","Qualitative","None"), # CHANGE THIS
      labels = c("Quantitative","Qualitative","Data type\nnot identified")
    ),
    result_effect = factor(
      result_effect,
      levels = c("Positive","Negative","Neutral","None"), # CHANGE THIS
      labels = c("Adaptive","Mal-adaptive","Neutral","Effect\nnot identified")
    )
    ) %>%
  filter(result_effect != "Effect\nnot identified", data_type != "Data type\nnot identified") %>%
  mutate(
    result_effect = droplevels(result_effect),
    data_type = droplevels(data_type)
  )


outcome_pal <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(length(levels(type_outcome_joined$outcome_type))-1,"Dark2")
outcome_pal <- c(outcome_pal, "grey")

#oro_type, outcome_type, data_type, result_effect
outcome_ggp <- ggplot(type_outcome_joined, aes(x=oro_type, y=weightedSum, fill = outcome_type))+
  facet_grid(result_effect~data_type, scales = "free_y")+
  geom_col(position="stack")+
  labs(
    x = "ORO type",
    y="N articles (weighted)",
    fill = "Outcome type"
  )+
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = outcome_pal,
    breaks = levels(type_outcome_joined$outcome_type)
  )+
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10)+
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1),
    plot.margin = unit(c(0.2, 0.5, 1, 1.5), units = "cm")
  )

outcome_ggp

ggsave(
  here::here("figures/supplemental/outcome_types_barplot.pdf"),
  plot = outcome_ggp,
  width = 7, height=6
)

```


# Clustering based on features (excl study results)

```{r prepare categorical data for clustering}
# Prepare data as factors
df_categories <- join_oro_with_multilabels_long(
  oro_df = oro_df,
  codedVars = codedVars,
  label_cols = c("continent", "climatic_impact_driver",
                 "spatial_scale","procedural_equity","governance_body",
                 "rules_of_law"),
  label_levels_list = list(
    continent = c("Africa","Asia", "Europe",
                  "North America","South America",
                  "Oceania"),
    climatic_impact_driver = cidAes$levels,
    spatial_scale = c("Local","National","Regional","Global","None"),
    procedural_equity = c("TRUE","FALSE"),
    governance_body = c("Community-based","National government",
                        "Regional (e.g. RFMOs)","International",
                        "Private organizations/companies"),
    rules_of_law = c("Law (binding)","Non-binding")
    
  ),
  id_col = "id"
) %>%
  filter(!if_any(everything(), ~ grepl("None", .x))) %>%
  mutate(
    procedural_equity = plyr::revalue(procedural_equity, c("TRUE"="Procedural equity", "FALSE"="No Procedural equity"))
  )%>%
  mutate(across(
      where(is.factor),
      ~droplevels(.x)
    )) %>%
  dplyr::select(-id)

summary(df_categories)
```


```{r multiple correspondance analysis - like PCA for categorical}
library(FactoMineR)
library(factoextra)


# MCA (no missing values allowed)
res_mca <- MCA(df_categories, graph = FALSE)

# Scree plot of eigenvalues
fviz_screeplot(res_mca, addlabels = TRUE)
# Dim 1 is 11.5 % of variation, Dim 2 8,7%

# MCA plot of individuals (data points)
mcaind <- fviz_mca_ind(res_mca,
             label = "none", # hide point labels
             addEllipses = TRUE, ellipse.type = "confidence")+
  labs(
    title=""
  )

mcaind


# MCA plot of variable levels
fviz_mca_var(res_mca, repel = TRUE)

```

```{r cluster each label based on MCA coordinates}
var_coords <- as.data.frame(res_mca$var$coord)
var_coords$varname <- rownames(var_coords)


set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility
km_vars <- kmeans(var_coords[, 1:2], centers = 7)
var_coords$cluster <- factor(km_vars$cluster)

fviz_cluster(km_vars, data = var_coords[, 1:2],
             geom = "text",
             labelsize = 4,
             main = "Clusters of Variable Levels")




mca_cluster_label_ggp <- ggplot(var_coords, aes(x = `Dim 1`, y = `Dim 2`, color = cluster)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(aes(label = varname), vjust = -0.7, size = 2.5,show.legend = FALSE, max.overlaps = 50, force=15, direction="both") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  coord_cartesian(clip="off")+
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2")+
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult=c(0.5,0.1)))+
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult=c(0.5,0.1)))+
  labs(
    color = "Label\n cluster",
    x = paste0("Dim 1 (", scales::number(res_mca$eig[1,2], accuracy = 0.1),"% var)"),
    y = paste0("Dim 2 (", scales::number(res_mca$eig[2,2], accuracy = 0.1),"% var)")
  )+
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

mca_cluster_label_ggp

combined <- wrap_plots(
  list(mcaind, mca_cluster_label_ggp), ncol=2
)+
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a")


ggsave(
  filename=here::here("figures/supplemental/mca_clustering_variable_labels.pdf"),
  plot = mca_cluster_label_ggp,
  width = 6, height=5, units='in'
)

ggsave(
  filename=here::here("figures/supplemental/mca_clustering_individual_and_variableLabels.pdf"),
  plot = combined,
  width = 10, height=5, units='in'
)


```


# Bivariate map governance indicators ~ vulnerability

```{r maps of all wbi governance indicators}
source(here::here("R", "functions_to_format.R")) # all functions needed to format data
source(here::here("R", "functions_to_plot.R")) # all functions needed to plot data


wbi_all <- readxl::read_excel(
  here::here("data/raw-data/external/worldbank_worldwide-governance-indicators/wgidataset.xlsx")
)%>%
  group_by(code, indicator) %>%
  summarise(
    year_sum = sum(as.numeric(estimate), na.rm=T),
    year_mean = mean(as.numeric(estimate), na.rm=T)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    sum_scaled = scales::rescale(year_sum),
    mean_scaled = scales::rescale(year_mean)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

wbi_combined <- wbi_all %>%
  group_by(code) %>%
  summarise(
    governance_indicator_sum = sum(sum_scaled, na.rm=T),
    governance_indicator_mean = sum(mean_scaled, na.rm=T)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    indicator = "Combined metric"
  )
  


# combined data frame
wbi_plt_df <- wbi_all %>%
  rename(value = mean_scaled)%>%
  select(code, indicator, value) %>%
  bind_rows(
    wbi_combined %>% 
      rename(value = governance_indicator_mean) %>%
      select(code, indicator, value)
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols = code, names_from = indicator, values_from = value
  )


indicators <- c(
  "cc" = "Control of corruption",
  "ge" = "Government effectiveness",
  "pv" = "Political stability & absence of violence/terrorism",
  "rl" = "Rule of law",
  "rq" = "Regulatory quality",
  "va" = "Voice and accountability",
  "Combined metric" = "Combined metric"
)



## read in external data
PROJ = "+proj=robin +lon_0=0 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +ellps=WGS84 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs"
world_shp <- ne_countries(returnclass = "sf", scale="large")

# Join in plotting data (fill and point)
data_map <- world_shp %>%
  dplyr::full_join(wbi_plt_df, by = c("iso_a3_eh"="code")) %>%
  sf::st_transform(crs = PROJ)

# check
world_shp %>% filter(adm0_a3 == "FRA") %>% select(adm0_a3, iso_a3_eh)
wbi_plt_df%>% filter(code=="FRA")%>% select(`Combined metric`)
data_map %>% filter(adm0_a3=="FRA") %>% select(`Combined metric`)

# reproject
data_map <- try_format_data2map(input_args = list(
  data = data_map,
  PROJ = PROJ
  )
)
# check
data_map$data %>% filter(adm0_a3=="FRA") %>% select(`Combined metric`)

## Plot
wbi_ggps <- list()

for(i in 1:length(indicators)){
  
  tmp_map <- ggplot2::ggplot() +
      ## Country fill
      ggplot2::geom_sf(data    =  data_map$data, 
                       mapping = ggplot2::aes(fill     = !! sym(names(indicators)[i]),
                                              geometry = geometry), 
                       color   = "black", 
                       size    = 0.1) +
      
      ggplot2::scale_fill_viridis_c(
        name = str_wrap(indicators[i], width = 30)
      )+
      # Bounding box
      ggplot2::geom_sf(data   = data_map$box, 
                       colour = "black", 
                       fill   = NA, 
                       size   = 0.1) +
      ## Add latitude and longitude labels
      ggplot2::geom_text(data = data_map$lat_text, mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = X.prj2-1*10e5, y = Y.prj,          label = lbl), color = "grey20", size = 1.5) +
      ggplot2::geom_text(data = data_map$lon_text, mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = X.prj,         y = Y.prj-0.5*10e5, label = lbl), color = "black",  size = 1.5) + 
      
      ggplot2::theme_void() +
    # Themes
    theme(axis.title.x     = element_blank(),
          axis.title.y     = element_blank(),
          plot.margin = unit(c(0.1,0.1,0.2,0.1),"cm"),
          legend.title.position = "top",
          legend.position = "bottom"
          )
  

    # tmp_map
  wbi_ggps[[i]] <- tmp_map
  
  
}


## combine 
require(patchwork)
combined_plots <- wrap_plots(
  wbi_ggps, ncol = 2
)+
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a")


ggsave(here::here("figures/supplemental/all_wbi_indicator_maps.pdf"),
       combined_plots, width = 8, height = 12)

ggsave(here::here("figures/supplemental/all_wbi_indicator_maps.png"),
       combined_plots, width = 8, height = 12, dpi=600)


```


```{r calculate correlation between governance and vulnerability}

## Shapiro-Wilk test for normality
## Null hypothesis -- data are normal
shapiro.test(all_country_data$governance_indicator) # p-value = 0.1518 -- normal
shapiro.test(all_country_data$fishery_vulnerability) # p-value = 0.01322 -- normal



cor.test(all_country_data$governance_indicator, all_country_data$fishery_vulnerability,
         method = "pearson")

# t = -3.757, df = 153, p-value = 0.000244
# alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
# 95 percent confidence interval:
#  -0.4286375 -0.1393653
# sample estimates:
#        cor 
# -0.2906282 

## Conclusion. Correlation is significant but not strong
```


```{r Bivariate map governance indicators ~ vulnerability}
library(R.utils)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(viridis)
library(broom)
library(raster)
library(rnaturalearth)

source(here::here("R", "functions_to_format.R")) # all functions needed to format data
source(here::here("R", "functions_to_plot.R")) # all functions needed to plot data


## read in external data
world_shp <- ne_countries(returnclass = "sf", scale="large")



## Calculate number of articles 

# check
id_country_data %>% filter(iso3c=="FRA")
anti_id_country_data %>% filter(iso3c=="FRA")

# Cut axes variables by quantile
dat_bivar <- id_country_data %>%
  mutate(
    group_x = cut(
      fishery_vulnerability,
      breaks = fv_cuts, quantile(fishery_vulnerability, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.1), na.rm = TRUE),
      include.lowest = TRUE,
      labels = FALSE
    ),
    group_y = cut(
      governance_indicator,
      breaks = quantile(governance_indicator, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.1), na.rm = TRUE),
      include.lowest = TRUE,
      labels = FALSE
    ),
    group   = paste0(group_x, ".", group_y)
  ) 

# Lookup table of group id by country
dat_bivar_iso <- dat_bivar %>%
  distinct(iso3c, group_x, group_y)

# Calculate number of articles by quantile group (to plot on the legend)
dat_bivar_sum <- dat_bivar%>%
  group_by(group_x, group_y, group) %>%
  summarise(
    n_articles = n_distinct(id)
  )

# Calculate number of articles by country (to plot on the map)
dat_country_sum <- dat_bivar%>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  summarise(
    n_articles_country = n_distinct(id)
  )


  



## Format data for colour scale

bivariate_color_scale <- color_bivariate_map(nquantiles  = 10, 
                                                 bottomright   = "#05A3FC", # rgb(130,0,80, maxColorValue = 255), 
                                             
                                                 upperright  = "#9D9F9F", # rgb(255,230,15, maxColorValue = 255),
                                                 bottomleft  = "#9D9F9F",
                                             
                                                 upperleft = "#F60497", # rgb(0,150,235, maxColorValue = 255)  
                                                 ylab        = "Governance indicator",
                                                 xlab        = "Fishery vulnerability")

bivariate_color_scale_plt <- bivariate_color_scale |> 
    separate(col = group, into = c("group_x", "group_y"), sep = "\\.", convert = TRUE, remove = FALSE)

dat_bivar_scale <- dat_bivar_sum %>%
  left_join(bivariate_color_scale, by = "group")


# check
ggplot(bivariate_color_scale_plt, aes(group_x, group_y,fill=fill))+geom_tile()+scale_fill_identity()





## Define a common size scale for number of articles to be used between map and bivariate legend
scale_range <- range(c(dat_bivar_sum$n_articles, dat_country_sum$n_articles_country), na.rm=T)

size_brks <- labeling::extended(scale_range[1], scale_range[2], m = 3)
size_brks[size_brks==0] <- 1



## legend plot
bivar_map_legend <- ggplot2::ggplot() +
    
    ggplot2::geom_tile(data    = bivariate_color_scale_plt,
                       mapping = ggplot2::aes(group_x, group_y, fill = fill)) +
    # ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(labels = as.vector(x_quantile), breaks = as.vector(x_quantile)) +
    ggplot2::scale_fill_identity() +
    
    ggplot2::geom_point(data = dat_bivar_sum, mapping = aes(group_x, group_y, size = n_articles), shape = 21, show.legend = TRUE, fill = "grey30", alpha=0.8) +
    ggplot2::scale_size(breaks = size_brks, range = c(1, 4)) +
    
    ggplot2::labs(x = "Fishery vulnerability", y = "Governance indicator") +
    cowplot::theme_map() +
    guides(size = guide_legend(title = "# Articles", title.position = "right", title.hjust = 0.5, ncol = 1)) +
    ggplot2::theme(axis.title      = ggplot2::element_text(size = 11), 
                   axis.title.x    = ggplot2::element_text(margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 0,
                                                                                    r = 0,
                                                                                    b = 0,
                                                                                    l = 0)),
                   axis.title.y    = ggplot2::element_text(angle  = 90,
                                                           margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 0,
                                                                                    r = 5,
                                                                                    b = 0,
                                                                                    l = 0)),
                   legend.key      = element_rect(fill = 'transparent', colour = 'transparent'),
                   legend.position = "right",
                   legend.justification = "center",
                   legend.title         = element_text(size = 11, angle = -90),
                   legend.text = element_text(size=10),
                   legend.margin      = margin(0,0,0,0),
                   legend.box.spacing = unit(0, "pt"),
                   plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill  = "white", 
                                                           color = "transparent")) +
    ggplot2::coord_fixed()
bivar_map_legend



## Format data for map plotting

# Join in the corresponding colour for each country according to its gov indicator and vulnerability
# Join in its iso lookup so it can be joined with shape file
dat_bivar_map <- dat_bivar_sum %>%
  left_join(dat_bivar_scale, by= base::intersect(colnames(dat_bivar_sum),colnames(dat_bivar_scale))) %>%
  right_join(dat_bivar_iso, by = base::intersect(colnames(dat_bivar_sum),colnames(dat_bivar_iso))) %>%
  rename(
    iso_code = iso3c
  )

# Join in plotting data (fill and point)
world_shp_data <- world_shp %>%
  dplyr::full_join(dat_bivar_map, by = c("iso_a3_eh"="iso_code")) %>%
  left_join(dat_country_sum, by = c("iso_a3_eh"="iso3c"))%>%
  sf::st_transform(crs = PROJ)



# check
world_shp_data %>% filter(adm0_a3 == "FRA") %>% select(adm0_a3, iso_a3_eh, n_articles_country)




# Reproject and format world shapefile
PROJ = "+proj=robin +lon_0=0 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +ellps=WGS84 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs"
data_map <- try_format_data2map(input_args = list(
  data = world_shp_data,
  PROJ = PROJ
  )
)

# set NA value for fill
data_map$data$fill[is.na(data_map$data$fill)] <- "grey90"
    


## Plot the map
gov_fishV_bivar_map <- ggplot2::ggplot() +
    ## Country fill
    ggplot2::geom_sf(data    = data_map$data, 
                     mapping = ggplot2::aes(fill     = fill,
                                            geometry = geometry), 
                     color   = "black", 
                     size    = 0.1) +
    
    ggplot2::scale_fill_identity(na.value = "grey90") +
  ## Points for number of articles
  stat_sf_coordinates(data        = data_map$data,
                          mapping     = aes(size = n_articles_country),
                          geom = "point",
                          shape = 21,
                          fill      = "grey20",
                          colour = "black",
                      alpha=0.8,
                          show.legend = FALSE) +
      scale_size(breaks = size_brks, range = range(1,4))+
    # Bounding box
    ggplot2::geom_sf(data   = data_map$box, 
                     colour = "black", 
                     fill   = NA, 
                     size   = 0.1) +
    ## Add latitude and longitude labels
    ggplot2::geom_text(data = data_map$lat_text, mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = X.prj2-1*10e5, y = Y.prj,          label = lbl), color = "grey20", size = 1.5) +
    ggplot2::geom_text(data = data_map$lon_text, mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = X.prj,         y = Y.prj-0.5*10e5, label = lbl), color = "black",  size = 1.5) + 
    
    ggplot2::theme_void() +
  # Themes
  theme(axis.title.x     = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y     = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "none")




# gov_fishV_bivar_map
    

## Format panels with legend
map_bi <- cowplot::ggdraw() +
    cowplot::draw_plot(gov_fishV_bivar_map,    x = 0.0, y = 0.00, width = 0.65, height = 1.0) +
    cowplot::draw_plot(bivar_map_legend, x = 0.65, y = 0.3, width = 0.4, height = 0.4)


## Save plot
ggsave(here::here("figures/supplemental/governance_v_fisheryVulnerability_bivarMap.pdf"),
       plot = map_bi, width = 10, height = 5, device = "pdf")
```


```{r one bivariate map governance indicators ~ vulnerability with crosshatched knowledge gaps}
library(R.utils)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(viridis)
library(broom)
library(raster)
library(rnaturalearth)

source(here::here("R", "functions_to_format.R")) # all functions needed to format data
source(here::here("R", "functions_to_plot.R")) # all functions needed to plot data


## read in external data
world_shp <- ne_countries(returnclass = "sf", scale="large")



## Format data for colour scale
bivariate_color_scale <- color_bivariate_map(nquantiles  = 10, 
                                                 bottomright   = "#05A3FC", # rgb(130,0,80, maxColorValue = 255), 
                                             
                                                 upperright  = "#9D9F9F", # rgb(255,230,15, maxColorValue = 255),
                                                 bottomleft  = "#9D9F9F",
                                             
                                                 upperleft = "#F60497", # rgb(0,150,235, maxColorValue = 255)  
                                                 ylab        = "Governance indicator",
                                                 xlab        = "Fishery vulnerability")

# check
bivariate_color_scale_plt <- bivariate_color_scale |> 
    separate(col = group, into = c("group_x", "group_y"), sep = "\\.", convert = TRUE, remove = FALSE)

ggplot(bivariate_color_scale_plt, aes(group_x, group_y,fill=fill))+geom_tile()+scale_fill_identity()




## Calculate number of articles 

# Cut axes variables by quantile
fv_cuts <- quantile(all_country_data$fishery_vulnerability, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.1), na.rm = TRUE)
gi_cuts <- quantile(all_country_data$governance_indicator, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.1), na.rm = TRUE)

all_dat_bivar <- all_country_data %>%
  mutate(
    group_x = cut(
      fishery_vulnerability,
      breaks = fv_cuts, quantile(fishery_vulnerability, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.1), na.rm = TRUE),
      include.lowest = TRUE,
      labels = FALSE
    ),
    group_y = cut(
      governance_indicator,
      breaks = quantile(governance_indicator, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.1), na.rm = TRUE),
      include.lowest = TRUE,
      labels = FALSE
    ),
    group   = paste0(group_x, ".", group_y)
  ) %>% 
  left_join(bivariate_color_scale, by = "group")



# Lookup table of group id by country
all_dat_bivar_iso <- all_dat_bivar %>%
  distinct(iso3c, group_x, group_y)

# Calculate number of articles by quantile group (to plot on the legend)
dat_bivar_sum <- all_dat_bivar %>%
  right_join(country_lookup, by = "iso3c") %>%
  right_join(id_country, by = "country") %>%
  group_by(group_x, group_y, group, fill) %>%
  summarise(
    n_articles = n_distinct(id)
  )

# Calculate number of articles by country (to plot on the map)
dat_country_sum <- all_dat_bivar %>%
  right_join(country_lookup, by = "iso3c") %>%
  right_join(id_country, by = "country") %>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  summarise(
    n_articles_country = n_distinct(id)
  )





## Define a common size scale for number of articles to be used between map and bivariate legend
scale_range <- range(c(dat_bivar_sum$n_articles, dat_country_sum$n_articles_country), na.rm=T)

size_brks <- labeling::extended(scale_range[1], scale_range[2], m = 3)
size_brks[size_brks==0] <- 1



## legend plot
bivar_map_legend <- ggplot2::ggplot() +
    
    ggplot2::geom_tile(data    = bivariate_color_scale_plt,
                       mapping = ggplot2::aes(group_x, group_y, fill = fill)) +
    # ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(labels = as.vector(x_quantile), breaks = as.vector(x_quantile)) +
    ggplot2::scale_fill_identity() +
    
    ggplot2::geom_point(data = dat_bivar_sum, mapping = aes(group_x, group_y, size = n_articles), shape = 21, show.legend = TRUE, fill = "grey30", alpha=0.8) +
    ggplot2::scale_size(breaks = size_brks, range = c(1, 4)) +
    
    ggplot2::labs(x = "Fishery vulnerability", y = "Governance indicator") +
    cowplot::theme_map() +
    guides(size = guide_legend(title = "# Articles", title.position = "right", title.hjust = 0.5, ncol = 1)) +
    ggplot2::theme(axis.title      = ggplot2::element_text(size = 11), 
                   axis.title.x    = ggplot2::element_text(margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 0,
                                                                                    r = 0,
                                                                                    b = 0,
                                                                                    l = 0)),
                   axis.title.y    = ggplot2::element_text(angle  = 90,
                                                           margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 0,
                                                                                    r = 5,
                                                                                    b = 0,
                                                                                    l = 0)),
                   legend.key      = element_rect(fill = 'transparent', colour = 'transparent'),
                   legend.position = "right",
                   legend.justification = "center",
                   legend.title         = element_text(size = 11, angle = -90),
                   legend.text = element_text(size=10),
                   legend.margin      = margin(0,0,0,0),
                   legend.box.spacing = unit(0, "pt"),
                   plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill  = "white", 
                                                           color = "transparent")) +
    ggplot2::coord_fixed()
bivar_map_legend



## Format data for map plotting

# Join in the corresponding colour for each country according to its gov indicator and vulnerability
# Join in its iso lookup so it can be joined with shape file
dat_bivar_map <- dat_bivar_sum %>%
  # left_join(dat_bivar_scale, by= base::intersect(colnames(dat_bivar_sum),colnames(dat_bivar_scale))) %>%
  full_join(all_dat_bivar_iso, by = base::intersect(colnames(dat_bivar_sum),colnames(all_dat_bivar_iso))) %>%
  rename(
    iso_code = iso3c
  ) %>%
  inner_join(country_lookup, by=c("iso_code"="iso3c")) 

all_dat_bivar_map <- all_dat_bivar %>%
  rename(iso_code = iso3c)

# check
dat_bivar_map %>% filter(iso_code %in% c("FRA","ISL")) # empty
all_dat_bivar_map %>% filter(iso_code %in% c("FRA","ISL")) # present

# Join in plotting data (fill and point)
PROJ = "+proj=robin +lon_0=0 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +ellps=WGS84 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs"

world_shp_data <- world_shp %>%
  # Plotting colour for each country (fill)
  dplyr::full_join(dat_bivar_map, by = c("iso_a3_eh"="iso_code")) %>%
  # Number of articles/country (point size)
  left_join(dat_country_sum, by = c("iso_a3_eh"="iso3c"))%>%
  sf::st_transform(crs = PROJ)

all_world_shp_data <- world_shp %>%
  # Plotting colour for each country
  dplyr::full_join(all_dat_bivar_map, by = c("iso_a3_eh"="iso_code")) %>%
  sf::st_transform(crs = PROJ)

# check
world_shp %>% filter(iso_a3_eh=="FRA") %>% select(iso_a3, geometry)
all_dat_bivar_map%>%filter(iso_code=="ISL")
dat_bivar_map%>%filter(iso_code=="ISL")
world_shp_data %>% filter(iso_a3_eh%in%c("FRA","ISL")) %>% select(iso_a3, n_articles_country,fill)
all_world_shp_data %>% filter(iso_a3_eh%in%c("FRA","ISL")) %>% select(iso_a3, fill)


# Reproject and format world shapefile
PROJ = "+proj=robin +lon_0=0 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +ellps=WGS84 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs"
data_map <- try_format_data2map(input_args = list(
  data = world_shp_data,
  PROJ = PROJ
  )
)
all_data_map <- try_format_data2map(input_args = list(
  data = all_world_shp_data,
  PROJ = PROJ
  )
)

# set NA value for fill
data_map$data$fill[is.na(data_map$data$fill)] <- "grey90"
all_data_map$data$fill[is.na(all_data_map$data$fill)] <- "grey90"

# Determine which countries should be hatched
incl_countries <- unique(data_map$data$iso_a3_eh[0 < data_map$data$n_articles_country])
all_data_map$data$pattern <- ifelse(
  !(all_data_map$data$iso_a3_eh %in% incl_countries) & all_data_map$data$fill != "grey90",
  "pattern", "none")
all_data_map$data$pattern[all_data_map$data$iso_a3_eh == "ISL"]
    
grat_label_size = 3

## Plot the map
require(magick)
# remotes::install_github("coolbutuseless/ggpattern")
require(ggpattern)


gov_fishV_bivar_map_all <- ggplot2::ggplot() +
    ## Country fill
    ggpattern::geom_sf_pattern(data    = all_data_map$data, 
                     mapping = ggplot2::aes(fill     = fill,
                                            pattern_type = pattern,
                                            pattern_alpha = pattern,
                                            geometry = geometry), 
                     pattern = 'magick',
                     pattern_fill = "black",
                     pattern_aspect_ratio = 1.5,
                     color   = "black", 
                     size    = 0.1) +
    
    ggplot2::scale_fill_identity(na.value = "grey90") +
  ggpattern::scale_pattern_type_discrete(choices = c("gray100","crosshatch45"))+
  ggpattern::scale_pattern_alpha_discrete(range = c(1, 0.75))+
  ## Points for number of articles
  stat_sf_coordinates(data        = data_map$data,
                          mapping     = aes(size = n_articles_country),
                          geom = "point",
                          shape = 21,
                          fill      = "grey20",
                          colour = "black",
                      alpha=0.8,
                          show.legend = FALSE) +
      scale_size(breaks = size_brks, range = range(1,4))+
    # Bounding box
    ggplot2::geom_sf(data   = data_map$box, 
                     colour = "black", 
                     fill   = NA, 
                     size   = 0.1) +
    # ## Add latitude and longitude labels
    # ggplot2::geom_text(data = data_map$lat_text, mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = X.prj2-1*10e5, y = Y.prj,          label = lbl), color = "grey20", size = grat_label_size, hjust=1) +
    # ggplot2::geom_text(data = data_map$lon_text, mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = X.prj,         y = Y.prj-0.5*10e5, label = lbl), color = "black",  size = grat_label_size, angle= 45, hjust=1, vjust=1) + 
    
    ggplot2::theme_void() +
  coord_sf(clip="off")+
  # Themes
  theme(axis.title.x     = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y     = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = unit(c(0,0.1,0.1,0.1), "cm"),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(size=26)
        )









## Format panels with legend

map_bi_all <- cowplot::ggdraw() +
    cowplot::draw_plot(gov_fishV_bivar_map_all,   x = 0.0, y = 0.35, width = 1, height = 0.6) +
    cowplot::draw_plot(bivar_map_legend, x = 0.3, y = 0.0, width = 0.4, height = 0.4)


# save plot
ggsave(here::here("figures/main/governance_v_fisheryVulnerability_bivarMap_all.png"),
       plot = map_bi_all, width = 8, height = 7, device = "png", dpi = 350)


ggsave(here::here("figures/main/governance_v_fisheryVulnerability_bivarMap_all.pdf"),
       plot = map_bi_all, width = 8, height = 8, device = "pdf")

```



```{r two bivariate map governance indicators ~ vulnerability}
library(R.utils)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(viridis)
library(broom)
library(raster)
library(rnaturalearth)

source(here::here("R", "functions_to_format.R")) # all functions needed to format data
source(here::here("R", "functions_to_plot.R")) # all functions needed to plot data


## read in external data
world_shp <- ne_countries(returnclass = "sf", scale="large")



## Format data for colour scale
bivariate_color_scale <- color_bivariate_map(nquantiles  = 10, 
                                                 bottomright   = "#05A3FC", # rgb(130,0,80, maxColorValue = 255), 
                                             
                                                 upperright  = "#9D9F9F", # rgb(255,230,15, maxColorValue = 255),
                                                 bottomleft  = "#9D9F9F",
                                             
                                                 upperleft = "#F60497", # rgb(0,150,235, maxColorValue = 255)  
                                                 ylab        = "Governance indicator",
                                                 xlab        = "Fishery vulnerability")

# check
bivariate_color_scale_plt <- bivariate_color_scale |> 
    separate(col = group, into = c("group_x", "group_y"), sep = "\\.", convert = TRUE, remove = FALSE)

ggplot(bivariate_color_scale_plt, aes(group_x, group_y,fill=fill))+geom_tile()+scale_fill_identity()




## Calculate number of articles 

# Cut axes variables by quantile
fv_cuts <- quantile(all_country_data$fishery_vulnerability, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.1), na.rm = TRUE)
gi_cuts <- quantile(all_country_data$governance_indicator, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.1), na.rm = TRUE)

all_dat_bivar <- all_country_data %>%
  mutate(
    group_x = cut(
      fishery_vulnerability,
      breaks = fv_cuts, quantile(fishery_vulnerability, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.1), na.rm = TRUE),
      include.lowest = TRUE,
      labels = FALSE
    ),
    group_y = cut(
      governance_indicator,
      breaks = quantile(governance_indicator, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.1), na.rm = TRUE),
      include.lowest = TRUE,
      labels = FALSE
    ),
    group   = paste0(group_x, ".", group_y)
  ) %>% 
  left_join(bivariate_color_scale, by = "group")



# Lookup table of group id by country
all_dat_bivar_iso <- all_dat_bivar %>%
  distinct(iso3c, group_x, group_y)

# Calculate number of articles by quantile group (to plot on the legend)
dat_bivar_sum <- all_dat_bivar %>%
  right_join(country_lookup, by = "iso3c") %>%
  right_join(id_country, by = "country") %>%
  group_by(group_x, group_y, group, fill) %>%
  summarise(
    n_articles = n_distinct(id)
  )

# Calculate number of articles by country (to plot on the map)
dat_country_sum <- all_dat_bivar %>%
  right_join(country_lookup, by = "iso3c") %>%
  right_join(id_country, by = "country") %>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  summarise(
    n_articles_country = n_distinct(id)
  )





## Define a common size scale for number of articles to be used between map and bivariate legend
scale_range <- range(c(dat_bivar_sum$n_articles, dat_country_sum$n_articles_country), na.rm=T)

size_brks <- labeling::extended(scale_range[1], scale_range[2], m = 3)
size_brks[size_brks==0] <- 1



## legend plot
bivar_map_legend <- ggplot2::ggplot() +
    
    ggplot2::geom_tile(data    = bivariate_color_scale_plt,
                       mapping = ggplot2::aes(group_x, group_y, fill = fill)) +
    # ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(labels = as.vector(x_quantile), breaks = as.vector(x_quantile)) +
    ggplot2::scale_fill_identity() +
    
    ggplot2::geom_point(data = dat_bivar_sum, mapping = aes(group_x, group_y, size = n_articles), shape = 21, show.legend = TRUE, fill = "grey30", alpha=0.8) +
    ggplot2::scale_size(breaks = size_brks, range = c(1, 4)) +
    
    ggplot2::labs(x = "Fishery vulnerability", y = "Governance indicator") +
    cowplot::theme_map() +
    guides(size = guide_legend(title = "# Articles", title.position = "right", title.hjust = 0.5, ncol = 1)) +
    ggplot2::theme(axis.title      = ggplot2::element_text(size = 11), 
                   axis.title.x    = ggplot2::element_text(margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 0,
                                                                                    r = 0,
                                                                                    b = 0,
                                                                                    l = 0)),
                   axis.title.y    = ggplot2::element_text(angle  = 90,
                                                           margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 0,
                                                                                    r = 5,
                                                                                    b = 0,
                                                                                    l = 0)),
                   legend.key      = element_rect(fill = 'transparent', colour = 'transparent'),
                   legend.position = "right",
                   legend.justification = "center",
                   legend.title         = element_text(size = 11, angle = -90),
                   legend.text = element_text(size=10),
                   legend.margin      = margin(0,0,0,0),
                   legend.box.spacing = unit(0, "pt"),
                   plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill  = "white", 
                                                           color = "transparent")) +
    ggplot2::coord_fixed()
bivar_map_legend



## Format data for map plotting

# Join in the corresponding colour for each country according to its gov indicator and vulnerability
# Join in its iso lookup so it can be joined with shape file
dat_bivar_map <- dat_bivar_sum %>%
  # left_join(dat_bivar_scale, by= base::intersect(colnames(dat_bivar_sum),colnames(dat_bivar_scale))) %>%
  full_join(all_dat_bivar_iso, by = base::intersect(colnames(dat_bivar_sum),colnames(dat_bivar_iso))) %>%
  rename(
    iso_code = iso3c
  ) %>%
  inner_join(country_lookup, by=c("iso_code"="iso3c")) 

anti_dat_bivar_map <- all_dat_bivar %>%
  anti_join(country_lookup, by = "iso3c") %>%
  rename(iso_code = iso3c)

# check
dat_bivar_map %>% filter(iso_code %in% c("FRA","ISL"))
anti_dat_bivar_map %>% filter(iso_code %in% c("FRA","ISL"))

# Join in plotting data (fill and point)
world_shp_data <- world_shp %>%
  # Plotting colour for each country (fill)
  dplyr::full_join(dat_bivar_map, by = c("iso_a3"="iso_code")) %>%
  # Number of articles/country (point size)
  left_join(dat_country_sum, by = c("iso_a3"="iso3c"))%>%
  sf::st_transform(crs = PROJ)

anti_world_shp_data <- world_shp %>%
  # Plotting colour for each country
  dplyr::full_join(anti_dat_bivar_map, by = c("iso_a3"="iso_code")) %>%
  sf::st_transform(crs = PROJ)

# check
anti_dat_bivar_map%>%filter(iso_code=="ISL")
dat_bivar_map%>%filter(iso_code=="ISL")
world_shp_data %>% filter(iso_a3%in%c("FRA","ISL")) %>% select(iso_a3, n_articles_country,fill)
anti_world_shp_data %>% filter(iso_a3%in%c("FRA","ISL")) %>% select(iso_a3, fill)


# Reproject and format world shapefile
PROJ = "+proj=robin +lon_0=0 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +ellps=WGS84 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs"
data_map <- try_format_data2map(input_args = list(
  data = world_shp_data,
  PROJ = PROJ
  )
)
anti_data_map <- try_format_data2map(input_args = list(
  data = anti_world_shp_data,
  PROJ = PROJ
  )
)

# set NA value for fill
data_map$data$fill[is.na(data_map$data$fill)] <- "grey90"
anti_data_map$data$fill[is.na(anti_data_map$data$fill)] <- "grey90"
    
grat_label_size = 3

## Plot the map
gov_fishV_bivar_map <- ggplot2::ggplot() +
  ggtitle("a.")+
    ## Country fill
    ggplot2::geom_sf(data    = data_map$data, 
                     mapping = ggplot2::aes(fill     = fill,
                                            geometry = geometry), 
                     color   = "black", 
                     size    = 0.1) +
    
    ggplot2::scale_fill_identity(na.value = "grey90") +
  ## Points for number of articles
  stat_sf_coordinates(data        = data_map$data,
                          mapping     = aes(size = n_articles_country),
                          geom = "point",
                          shape = 21,
                          fill      = "grey20",
                          colour = "black",
                      alpha=0.8,
                          show.legend = FALSE) +
      scale_size(breaks = size_brks, range = range(1,4))+
    # Bounding box
    ggplot2::geom_sf(data   = data_map$box, 
                     colour = "black", 
                     fill   = NA, 
                     size   = 0.1) +
    ## Add latitude and longitude labels
    ggplot2::geom_text(data = data_map$lat_text, mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = X.prj2-1*10e5, y = Y.prj,          label = lbl), color = "grey20", size = grat_label_size, hjust=1) +
    ggplot2::geom_text(data = data_map$lon_text, mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = X.prj,         y = Y.prj-0.5*10e5, label = lbl), color = "black",  size = grat_label_size, angle= 45, hjust=1, vjust=1) + 
    
    ggplot2::theme_void() +
  coord_sf(clip="off")+
  # Themes
  theme(axis.title.x     = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y     = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = unit(c(0,0.1,0.1,0.1), "cm"),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(size=26)
        )



# gov_fishV_bivar_map



## Plot the ant-map
anti_gov_fishV_bivar_map <- ggplot2::ggplot() +
  ggtitle("b.")+
    ## Country fill
    ggplot2::geom_sf(data    = anti_data_map$data, 
                     mapping = ggplot2::aes(fill     = fill,
                                            geometry = geometry), 
                     color   = "black", 
                     size    = 0.1) +
    
    ggplot2::scale_fill_identity(na.value = "grey90") +
    # Bounding box
    ggplot2::geom_sf(data   = data_map$box, 
                     colour = "black", 
                     fill   = NA, 
                     size   = 0.1) +
    ## Add latitude and longitude labels
    ggplot2::geom_text(data = data_map$lat_text, mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = X.prj2-1*10e5, y = Y.prj,          label = lbl), color = "grey20", size = grat_label_size, hjust=1) +
    ggplot2::geom_text(data = data_map$lon_text, mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = X.prj,         y = Y.prj-0.5*10e5, label = lbl), color = "black",  size = grat_label_size, angle= 45, hjust=1, vjust=1) + 
    
    ggplot2::theme_void() +
  coord_sf(clip="off")+
  # Themes
  theme(axis.title.x     = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y     = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = unit(c(0,0.1,0.1,0.1), "cm"),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(size=26)
        )




# anti_gov_fishV_bivar_map
    

## Format panels with legend
patchwork_layout <- c(
  "AAA
  AAA
  #B#
  CCC
  CCC"
)

map_bi <- wrap_plots(
  gov_fishV_bivar_map, free(bivar_map_legend), anti_gov_fishV_bivar_map,
  ncol=1, nrow=3,
  design = patchwork_layout
  # widths = c(1), heights = c(1, 0.4, 1)
  # guides = "keep", axes="keep"
)

## Save plot
ggsave(here::here("figures/main/governance_v_fisheryVulnerability_bivarMap_anti.png"), 
       plot=map_bi,
       width = 10, height = 12, dpi=300)

ggsave(here::here("figures/main/governance_v_fisheryVulnerability_bivarMap_anti.pdf"), 
       plot=map_bi,
       width = 10, height = 12)
```



Only governance indicator predicts the number of articles
```{r model fits of the number of articles ~ governance + vulnerability}
require(glmmTMB)
require(ggeffects)
require(sjPlot)

dat_bivar_mod_df <- dat_bivar %>%
  group_by(country, governance_indicator, fishery_vulnerability) %>%
  summarise(
    n_articles = n_distinct(id),
    .groups = "drop"
  )%>%
  filter(0 < n_articles)


bivar_mod <- glm(n_articles ~ governance_indicator + fishery_vulnerability, 
                 data = dat_bivar_mod_df,
                 family=poisson())
od <- performance::check_overdispersion(bivar_mod)

if(od$dispersion_ratio > 1 & od$p_value < 0.05){
    bivar_mod <- glmmTMB(n_articles ~ governance_indicator + fishery_vulnerability,
             data = dat_bivar_mod_df,
             family=nbinom2)
    
}


bivar_mod_sum <- summary(bivar_mod)
bivar_mod_sum

r2 <- performance::r2_mcfadden(bivar_mod)

modSummary <- as.data.frame(bivar_mod_sum$coefficients$cond) %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("Term") %>%
    mutate(
      Term = str_to_sentence(gsub("_"," ", Term)),
      sig = ifelse(`Pr(>|z|)` <= 0.05, "*",""),
      `P value class` = paste0( ufs::formatPvalue(`Pr(>|z|)`),sig),
      label = paste0("\u03b2 = ", signif(Estimate, 2),"\n",`P value class`),
      `R2 (McFadden)` = signif(r2$R2, 3)
    ) %>%
  select(-sig)

gov_ggp <- plot_model(bivar_mod, type = "pred", terms = c("governance_indicator"),show.data = TRUE)+
  geom_text(x=-Inf, y=Inf, label = modSummary$label[grepl("Govern", modSummary$Term)],
            inherit.aes = FALSE, vjust=1, hjust=0,size=3, check_overlap = TRUE)+
  labs(
    x="Governance indicator",
    y="N articles"
  )+
  theme_minimal(base_size = 9)+
  theme(
    plot.title = element_blank()
  )
gov_ggp

vul_ggp <- plot_model(bivar_mod, type = "pred", terms = c("fishery_vulnerability"),show.data = TRUE)+
  geom_text(x=-Inf, y=Inf, label = modSummary$label[grepl("Fish", modSummary$Term)],
            inherit.aes = FALSE, vjust=1, hjust=0,size=3, check_overlap = TRUE)+
  labs(
    x="Fishery vulnerability",
    y="N articles"
  )+
  theme_minimal(base_size = 9)+
  theme(
    plot.title = element_blank()
  )
vul_ggp




## Save results

# Plot
require(patchwork)
combined <- wrap_plots(list(gov_ggp, vul_ggp), ncol = 2) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'a')

ggsave(here::here("figures/supplemental/glm_nArticles_by_govInd_and_fishVul.pdf"),
        combined, width = 7, height = 3.5)
ggsave(here::here("figures/supplemental/glm_nArticles_by_govInd_and_fishVul.png"),
        combined, width = 7, height = 3.5, dpi=600)

# Model summary
write.csv(
  modSummary %>% select(-label),
  here::here("outputs/glm_nArticles_by_govInd_and_fishVul_mod_summary.csv"),row.names = FALSE
)

```


# Outcomes

Model is biased to pick up more positive effects, so not sure how good the finding is that there are less negative...

Instead, focus on predictors of positive outcomes.

- Of the positive effects, show how many are qualitative vs quantitative by oro type

- model predicting positive effect predictors: continent, oro type, data_type, procedural_equity, governance, spatial_scale (dont fit binary for mixed effects because these could cancel out...)


```{r format binary and all outcome model data}

# calculate outcomes
oroOutcomes <- expand_labels_with_weight(df = studyResults, id_col = article_outcome_id, list_col = oro_type) %>%
  left_join(studyResults %>% select(id, article_outcome_id,result_type, result_effect)) %>%
  left_join(codedVars %>% select(id, evidence_type), by = "id") %>%
  rename(
    oro_type = label
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    oro_type = factor(
      oro_type,
      levels = oroTypeAes$levels
    ),
    evidence_type = factor(
      evidence_type,
      levels=c("Quantitative metric","Social empirical","Qualitative - other")
    )
  )


# ## expand result_type [social, ecological, etc..] labels
# studyResults_bin <- expand_labels(studyResults,id_col = id, list_col = result_type) %>%
#   select(id, parsed_labels, result_effect_regrouped)%>%
#   rename(result_type = parsed_labels, adaptive = result_effect_regrouped) %>%
#   group_by(id, result_type, adaptive)%>%
#   mutate(
#     adaptive = factor(
#       adaptive,
#       levels = c("Other","Adaptive") # success is the second level for binomial
#     )
#   )

# Or maybe right now don't worry about result type and just do by publication id and adaptive effect P/A
allIds <- data.frame(id = unique(codedVars$id))
studyResults_bin <- allIds %>%
  left_join(
    studyResults %>% 
      filter(result_effect_regrouped == "Adaptive") %>%
      rename(adaptive = result_effect_regrouped) %>%
      select(id, adaptive),
    by = "id"
  ) %>%
  replace_na(list(adaptive = "None identified"))%>%
  mutate(
    adaptive = factor(
      adaptive,
      levels = c("None identified","Adaptive") # success is the second level for binomial
    )
  )


## alternate data set at the outcome level. So possible to have > 1 outcomes /publication. 
result_model_df <- join_oro_with_multilabels_long(
  oro_df = oro_df,
  codedVars = codedVars,
  label_cols = c("continent", "climatic_impact_driver",
                 "spatial_scale", "time_scale","procedural_equity","governance_body",
                 "rules_of_law"),
  label_levels_list = list(
    continent = c("Africa","Asia", "Europe",
                  "North America","South America",
                  "Oceania"),
    climatic_impact_driver = cidAes$levels,
    spatial_scale = c("Local","National","Regional","Global","None"),
```
