```{r format binary and all outcome model data}
    time_scale = c("<= 1 month","1 month <= 1 year","1 year <= 5 years","5 years <= 10 years","> 10 years","None"),
    procedural_equity = c("TRUE","FALSE"),
    governance_body = c("Community-based","National government",
                        "Regional (e.g. RFMOs)","International",
                        "Private organizations/companies"),
    rules_of_law = c("Law (binding)","Non-binding")
    
  ),
  id_col = "id"
) %>%
  full_join(
    studyResults_bin,
    by="id",
    relationship = "many-to-many"
  ) %>%
  # replace_na(list(adaptive = "Adaptive"))%>%
  mutate(across(
      where(is.factor),
      ~droplevels(.x)
    ))

summary(result_model_df)



```




## Binary Gradient-boosted tree model of adaptive outcomes (at the outcome level)

However, when all are fit together, which variables are most influential in predicting adaptive outcomes? Use Gradient boosted tree model

```{r gradient boosted tree model}
library(gbm)

# fit the model
set.seed(123)
result_gbm_df <- result_model_df %>%
  mutate(
    adaptive = as.numeric(adaptive)-1
  ) %>%
  filter(!if_any(everything(), ~ grepl("None", .))) %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      where(is.factor),
      ~droplevels(.x)
    )
  )%>%
  # Add country indicators
  left_join(id_country_data %>% 
              select(-c(country, country_weight, iso3c)), 
            by="id", relationship = "many-to-many")%>%
  select(-id) 


result_gbm_df$result_type = NULL
result_gbm_df$climatic_impact_driver = NULL

## Subdivide into test and training sets
set.seed(123)
sub <- sample(2,nrow(result_gbm_df),replace=TRUE,prob=c(0.7,0.3))
result_gbm_df.train <- result_gbm_df[sub==1,]
result_gbm_df.test <- result_gbm_df[sub==2,]


gbm_fit <- gbm(adaptive~.,distribution="bernoulli",data=result_gbm_df.train,
           n.trees=1000,interaction.depth=3,shrinkage=0.05,cv.folds=3)

## Select the optimal number of trees by cross validation
best.iter <- gbm.perf(gbm_fit,method="cv")


# The summary shows the variable importance
gbm_summary <- summary(gbm_fit,n.trees=best.iter) %>%
  as.data.frame()%>%
  rename(Variable = var, `Relative influence`= rel.inf) %>%
  mutate(
    `Variable name`=stringr::str_to_sentence(gsub("_"," ", Variable)),
    `Variable name` = case_when(
      grepl("ORO ", `Variable name`, ignore.case=TRUE) ~ "Adaptation option",
      TRUE ~ `Variable name`
    )
  )
  
gbm_summary 

write.csv(gbm_summary%>%
            select(`Variable name`, `Relative influence`),
          file = here::here("outputs/adaptive_gbm_model_summary.csv"))


## Partial dependence plots ? Or stick to the glm because it shows significance?

# Generate ggplots from gbm partial dependencies
gbm_plot_list <- lapply(1:nrow(gbm_summary), function(i) {
  var_name <- gbm_summary$Variable[i]
  var_name_sym <- rlang::sym(var_name)
  var_label <- gbm_summary$`Variable name`[i]
  relInfluence <- gbm_summary$`Relative influence`[i]%>%
    scales::number(cale_cut = scales::cut_short_scale())
  relInfluence <- paste("Relative influence =", relInfluence)
  
  if(class(result_gbm_df.train[,var_name])=="numeric"){
    ## If numeric
    pd_data <- plot.gbm(gbm_fit, var_name, n.trees = best.iter, return.grid = TRUE, type="response") %>%
      mutate(
        x = as.numeric(paste0(!!var_name_sym))
        
      )%>%
      arrange(!!var_name_sym)
    
    
    n_samples <- data.frame(x=result_gbm_df[,var_name])
    sample_dens <- ggplot()+ geom_density(data = n_samples, aes(x=x), inherit.aes = FALSE)
    sample_dens <- layer_data(sample_dens)
    sample_dens$density_scaled <- scales::rescale(
      sample_dens$density,
      to = range(pd_data$y, na.rm=T)
    )
    
    # Create ggplot
    ggplot(pd_data, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
      geom_ribbon(
        data = sample_dens,
        aes(x=x, ymax = density_scaled),
        fill="grey", alpha=0.5, ymin = min(pd_data$y, na.rm=T)
      )+
      geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth=1) +
      geom_text(
        x = -Inf, label = relInfluence, y=Inf, hjust = 0, vjust=1,
        size=3
      )+
      labs(
        title = var_label,
        y = "Pr of adaptive outcome",
        x=""
      ) +
      scale_y_continuous(
        expand = expansion(mult = c(0,0.3)),
        labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01)
      )+
      coord_cartesian(clip="off")+
      theme_minimal(base_size = 10)+
      theme(
        axis.title = element_text(size=9),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size=9),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust=1, size=9)
      )
    
  }else{
    
    ## If categorical
    n_samples <- table(result_gbm_df.train[,var_name]) %>% 
    as.data.frame() %>%
    rename_with(~ var_name, .cols = "Var1")
    
    # recalculate counts of n articles from original data 
    if(var_name == "oro_type"){
      
      for(s in 1:nrow(n_samples)){
        oroSamp <- oro_df %>%
          filter(grepl(as.character(n_samples[s,var_name]), oro_type)) %>%
          summarise(
            n = n_distinct(id)
          )
        n_samples$Freq[s] <- oroSamp$n
      }
      
    }else{
      for(s in 1:nrow(n_samples)){
        n_samples$Freq[s] <- sum(grepl(as.character(n_samples[s,var_name]), codedVars[[var_name]], fixed = TRUE))
      }
    }
    
    
    
  
    pd_data <- plot.gbm(gbm_fit, var_name, n.trees = best.iter, return.grid = TRUE, type="response") %>%
      left_join(n_samples, by = var_name) %>%
      mutate(
        xlabel = paste0(!!var_name_sym, "\n(n = ", Freq,")")
      )%>%
      arrange(!!var_name_sym)
    pd_data$xlabel <- factor(
      pd_data$xlabel,
      levels = pd_data$xlabel[order(pd_data[,var_name])]
    )
    
    
    # Create ggplot
    ggplot(pd_data, aes(x = xlabel, y = y)) +
      geom_point(color = "steelblue", size=3) +
      geom_text(
        x = -Inf, label = relInfluence, y=Inf, hjust = 0, vjust=1,
        size=3
      )+
      labs(
        title = var_label,
        y = "Pr of adaptive outcome",
        x=""
      ) +
      # scale_x_discrete(
      #   labels = function(x){
      #     str_wrap(gsub("/"," / ", x), width = 35)
      #     }
      # )+
      scale_y_continuous(
        expand = expansion(mult = c(0,0.3)),
        labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01)
      )+
      coord_cartesian(clip="off")+
      theme_minimal(base_size = 10)+
      theme(
        axis.title = element_text(size=9),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size=9),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust=1, size=9)
      )
  } ## End of if categorical
  
  
})

# Combine using patchwork
combined_gbm_plot <- wrap_plots(gbm_plot_list, ncol = 3) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'a')

# Save to PDF
ggsave(here::here("figures/main/gbm_partial_dependence_plots.pdf"),
       combined_gbm_plot, width = 12, height = 11)

ggsave(here::here("figures/main/gbm_partial_dependence_plots.png"),
       combined_gbm_plot, width = 12, height = 11, dpi=600)


## Assess on the test set
pr <- predict(gbm_fit, newdata=result_gbm_df.test, type="response") > 0.5

cm <- caret::confusionMatrix(as.factor(as.numeric(pr)), reference = as.factor(result_gbm_df.test$adaptive))

cm[["byClass"]][c("F1","Recall","Precision")]
       # F1    Recall Precision 
       #  1         1         1
```

```{r gradient boosted tree model - incl climatic impact driver}
library(gbm)

# fit the model
set.seed(123)
result_gbm_df <- result_model_df %>%
  mutate(
    adaptive = as.numeric(adaptive)-1
  ) %>%
  filter(!if_any(everything(), ~ grepl("None", .))) %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      where(is.factor),
      ~droplevels(.x)
    )
  )%>%
  # Add country indicators
  left_join(id_country_data %>% 
              select(-c(country, country_weight, iso3c)), 
            by="id", relationship = "many-to-many")%>%
  select(-id) 


result_gbm_df$result_type = NULL
# result_gbm_df$climatic_impact_driver = NULL

## Subdivide into test and training sets
set.seed(123)
sub <- sample(2,nrow(result_gbm_df),replace=TRUE,prob=c(0.7,0.3))
result_gbm_df.train <- result_gbm_df[sub==1,]
result_gbm_df.test <- result_gbm_df[sub==2,]


gbm_fit <- gbm(adaptive~.,distribution="bernoulli",data=result_gbm_df.train,
           n.trees=1000,interaction.depth=3,shrinkage=0.05,cv.folds=3)

## Select the optimal number of trees by cross validation
best.iter <- gbm.perf(gbm_fit,method="cv")


# The summary shows the variable importance
gbm_summary <- summary(gbm_fit,n.trees=best.iter) %>%
  as.data.frame()%>%
  rename(Variable = var, `Relative influence`= rel.inf) %>%
  mutate(
    `Variable name`=stringr::str_to_sentence(gsub("_"," ", Variable)),
    `Variable name` = case_when(
      grepl("ORO ", `Variable name`, ignore.case=TRUE) ~ "Adaptation option",
      TRUE ~ `Variable name`
    )
  )
  
gbm_summary 

write.csv(gbm_summary%>%
            select(`Variable name`, `Relative influence`),
          file = here::here("outputs/adaptive_gbm_model_summary_2.csv"))


## Partial dependence plots ? Or stick to the glm because it shows significance?

# Generate ggplots from gbm partial dependencies
gbm_plot_list <- lapply(1:nrow(gbm_summary), function(i) {
  var_name <- gbm_summary$Variable[i]
  var_name_sym <- rlang::sym(var_name)
  var_label <- gbm_summary$`Variable name`[i]
  relInfluence <- gbm_summary$`Relative influence`[i]%>%
    scales::number(cale_cut = scales::cut_short_scale())
  relInfluence <- paste("Relative influence =", relInfluence)
  
  if(class(result_gbm_df.train[,var_name])=="numeric"){
    ## If numeric
    pd_data <- plot.gbm(gbm_fit, var_name, n.trees = best.iter, return.grid = TRUE, type="response") %>%
      mutate(
        x = as.numeric(paste0(!!var_name_sym))
        
      )%>%
      arrange(!!var_name_sym)
    
    
    n_samples <- data.frame(x=result_gbm_df[,var_name])
    sample_dens <- ggplot()+ geom_density(data = n_samples, aes(x=x), inherit.aes = FALSE)
    sample_dens <- layer_data(sample_dens)
    sample_dens$density_scaled <- scales::rescale(
      sample_dens$density,
      to = range(pd_data$y, na.rm=T)
    )
    
    # Create ggplot
    ggplot(pd_data, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
      geom_ribbon(
        data = sample_dens,
        aes(x=x, ymax = density_scaled),
        fill="grey", alpha=0.5, ymin = min(pd_data$y, na.rm=T)
      )+
      geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth=1) +
      geom_text(
        x = -Inf, label = relInfluence, y=Inf, hjust = 0, vjust=1,
        size=3
      )+
      labs(
        title = var_label,
        y = "Pr of adaptive outcome",
        x=""
      ) +
      scale_y_continuous(
        expand = expansion(mult = c(0,0.3)),
        labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01)
      )+
      coord_cartesian(clip="off")+
      theme_minimal(base_size = 10)+
      theme(
        axis.title = element_text(size=9),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size=9),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust=1, size=9)
      )
    
  }else{
    
    ## If categorical
    n_samples <- table(result_gbm_df.train[,var_name]) %>% 
    as.data.frame() %>%
    rename_with(~ var_name, .cols = "Var1")
    
    # recalculate counts of n articles from original data 
    if(var_name == "oro_type"){
      
      for(s in 1:nrow(n_samples)){
        oroSamp <- oro_df %>%
          filter(grepl(as.character(n_samples[s,var_name]), oro_type)) %>%
          summarise(
            n = n_distinct(id)
          )
        n_samples$Freq[s] <- oroSamp$n
      }
      
    }else{
      for(s in 1:nrow(n_samples)){
        n_samples$Freq[s] <- sum(grepl(as.character(n_samples[s,var_name]), codedVars[[var_name]], fixed = TRUE))
      }
    }
    
    
    
  
    pd_data <- plot.gbm(gbm_fit, var_name, n.trees = best.iter, return.grid = TRUE, type="response") %>%
      left_join(n_samples, by = var_name) %>%
      mutate(
        xlabel = paste0(!!var_name_sym, "\n(n = ", Freq,")")
      )%>%
      arrange(!!var_name_sym)
    pd_data$xlabel <- factor(
      pd_data$xlabel,
      levels = pd_data$xlabel[order(pd_data[,var_name])]
    )
    
    
    # Create ggplot
    ggplot(pd_data, aes(x = xlabel, y = y)) +
      geom_point(color = "steelblue", size=3) +
      geom_text(
        x = -Inf, label = relInfluence, y=Inf, hjust = 0, vjust=1,
        size=3
      )+
      labs(
        title = var_label,
        y = "Pr of adaptive outcome",
        x=""
      ) +
      # scale_x_discrete(
      #   labels = function(x){
      #     str_wrap(gsub("/"," / ", x), width = 35)
      #     }
      # )+
      scale_y_continuous(
        expand = expansion(mult = c(0,0.3)),
        labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01)
      )+
      coord_cartesian(clip="off")+
      theme_minimal(base_size = 10)+
      theme(
        axis.title = element_text(size=9),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size=9),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust=1, size=9)
      )
  } ## End of if categorical
  
  
})

# Combine using patchwork
combined_gbm_plot <- wrap_plots(gbm_plot_list, ncol = 3) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'a')

# Save to PDF
ggsave(here::here("figures/main/gbm_partial_dependence_plots_2.pdf"),
       combined_gbm_plot, width = 10.5, height = 12)




## Assess on the test set
pr <- predict(gbm_fit, newdata=result_gbm_df.test, type="response") > 0.5

cm <- caret::confusionMatrix(as.factor(as.numeric(pr)), reference = as.factor(result_gbm_df.test$adaptive))

cm[["byClass"]][c("F1","Recall","Precision")]
#        F1    Recall Precision 
# 0.8888889 0.8000000 1.0000000 
```


## Result type priorities and trade offs plot


Rationale: are social outcomes (e.g. being able to stay fishing -- important to cultural identity) priotizied over economic costs?

```{r glm of unweighted trade offs}
# calculate outcomes
oroOutcomes <- expand_labels_with_weight(df = studyResults, id_col = article_outcome_id, list_col = oro_type) %>%
  left_join(studyResults %>% select(id, article_outcome_id,result_type, result_effect)) %>%
  left_join(codedVars %>% select(id, evidence_type), by = "id") %>%
  rename(
    oro_type = label
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    oro_type = factor(
      oro_type,
      levels = oroTypeAes$levels
    ),
    evidence_type = factor(
      evidence_type,
      levels=c("Quantitative metric","Social empirical","Qualitative - other")
    )
  )

tradeOffs <- expand_labels_with_weight(df = oroOutcomes, id_col = article_outcome_id, list_col = result_type) %>%
  left_join(oroOutcomes %>% select(article_outcome_id, id, oro_type, evidence_type, result_effect, weight) %>% rename(oro_weight = weight),
            relationship = "many-to-many") %>%
  rename(
    result_type = label,
    result_type_weight = weight
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    weight = oro_weight*result_type_weight,
    result_type = factor(
      result_type,
      levels = c("Management","Economic","Social","Ecological")
    )
  ) 
  # group_by(
  #   result_type, oro_type, result_effect
  # ) %>%
  # summarise(
  #   weightedSum = sum(weight, na.rm = T)
  # )

resultTypes <- levels(tradeOffs$result_type)
oros <- levels(tradeOffs$oro_type)
resultTradeOffs_df_uw <- data.frame()
for(r in 1:length(resultTypes)){
  # for(o in 1:length(oros)){
    
  
    posIds <- tradeOffs %>%
      filter(result_type == resultTypes[r], #oro_type == oros[o], 
             result_effect=="Positive")
    posIds <- unique(posIds$id)
    
    dfTemp <- tradeOffs %>%
      filter(id %in% posIds, 
             result_type != resultTypes[r], #oro_type == oros[o], 
             result_effect!="Neutral")
    if(nrow(dfTemp) < 1){
      next
    }
    dfTemp<- dfTemp%>%
      mutate(
        result_effect = factor(result_effect, levels = c("Positive","Negative"))
      ) %>%
      group_by(result_type, result_effect) %>%
      summarise(
        n = n()
      )%>%
      complete(result_effect, fill = list(weightedSum = NA)) %>%
      pivot_wider(
        names_from = result_effect,
        values_from = n
      ) %>%
      mutate(
        Negative = Negative*-1
      )%>%
      replace_na(list(Positive = 0, Negative = 0))%>%
      mutate(
        # oro_type = oros[o],
        positive_result = resultTypes[r]
      )
    
    resultTradeOffs_df_uw <- resultTradeOffs_df_uw %>%
      bind_rows(dfTemp)
    
  # }
}
resultTradeOffs_df_uw$positive_result <- factor(
  as.character(resultTradeOffs_df_uw$positive_result),
  levels = levels(resultTradeOffs_df_uw$result_type)
)


## model the trade off
tradeOff_int <- glm(cbind(Positive, abs(Negative))~ 1, data = resultTradeOffs_df_uw, family = "binomial")

summary(tradeOff_int)
# Coefficients:
#             Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
# (Intercept)   1.2476     0.1516   8.229   <2e-16 ***
# ---


## By ORO type -------------

oros <- levels(tradeOffs$oro_type)
resultTradeOffs_df_uw_o <- data.frame()
for(r in 1:length(resultTypes)){
  for(o in 1:length(oros)){
    
  
    posIds <- tradeOffs %>%
      filter(result_type == resultTypes[r], oro_type == oros[o], 
             result_effect=="Positive")
    posIds <- unique(posIds$id)
    
    dfTemp <- tradeOffs %>%
      filter(id %in% posIds, 
             result_type != resultTypes[r], oro_type == oros[o], 
             result_effect!="Neutral")
    if(nrow(dfTemp) < 1){
      next
    }
    dfTemp<- dfTemp%>%
      mutate(
        result_effect = factor(result_effect, levels = c("Positive","Negative"))
      ) %>%
      group_by(result_type, result_effect) %>%
      summarise(
        n = n()
      )%>%
      complete(result_effect, fill = list(weightedSum = NA)) %>%
      pivot_wider(
        names_from = result_effect,
        values_from = n
      ) %>%
      mutate(
        Negative = Negative*-1
      )%>%
      replace_na(list(Positive = 0, Negative = 0))%>%
      mutate(
        oro_type = oros[o],
        positive_result = resultTypes[r]
      )
    
    resultTradeOffs_df_uw_o <- resultTradeOffs_df_uw_o %>%
      bind_rows(dfTemp)
    
  }
}
resultTradeOffs_df_uw_o$positive_result <- factor(
  as.character(resultTradeOffs_df_uw$positive_result),
  levels = levels(resultTradeOffs_df_uw$result_type)
)

# sample size
sum(resultTradeOffs_df_uw_o$Positive)+sum(abs(resultTradeOffs_df_uw_o$Negative)) # 179
sum(resultTradeOffs_df_uw_o$Positive) #142
sum(abs(resultTradeOffs_df_uw_o$Negative)) #37

# model the trade off
tradeOff_int_o <- glm(cbind(Positive, abs(Negative))~ oro_type + 1, data = resultTradeOffs_df_uw_o, family = "binomial")

summary(tradeOff_int_o)

# ## Nothing significant except intercept
# Coefficients:
#                                         Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)  
# (Intercept)                               1.7918     0.7638   2.346    0.019 *
# oro_typeDisaster response technologies   -0.4055     1.3540  -0.299    0.765  
# oro_typeEconomic                         -0.8473     0.8842  -0.958    0.338  
# oro_typeFisheries management             -0.3365     0.8715  -0.386    0.699  
# oro_typeFishing technologies             15.7743  1978.0903   0.008    0.994  
# oro_typeOcean governance                 -0.1823     0.8851  -0.206    0.837  
# oro_typeSocio-behavioural                -1.0186     0.8397  -1.213    0.225  
# oro_typeSoft infrastructure               0.4055     1.0672   0.380    0.704



anova(tradeOff_int_o)
#          Df Deviance Resid. Df Resid. Dev Pr(>Chi)
# NULL                        53     40.033         
# oro_type  7   7.5515        46     32.482   0.3738

```


```{r barplot of trade offs}
# calculate outcomes
oroOutcomes <- expand_labels_with_weight(df = studyResults, id_col = article_outcome_id, list_col = oro_type) %>%
  left_join(studyResults %>% select(id, article_outcome_id,result_type, result_effect)) %>%
  left_join(codedVars %>% select(id, evidence_type), by = "id") %>%
  rename(
    oro_type = label
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    oro_type = factor(
      oro_type,
      levels = oroTypeAes$levels
    ),
    evidence_type = factor(
      evidence_type,
      levels=c("Quantitative metric","Social empirical","Qualitative - other")
    )
  )

tradeOffs <- expand_labels_with_weight(df = oroOutcomes, id_col = article_outcome_id, list_col = result_type) %>%
  left_join(oroOutcomes %>% select(article_outcome_id, id, oro_type, evidence_type, result_effect, weight) %>% rename(oro_weight = weight),
            relationship = "many-to-many") %>%
  rename(
    result_type = label,
    result_type_weight = weight
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    weight = oro_weight*result_type_weight,
    result_type = factor(
      result_type,
      levels = c("Management","Economic","Social","Ecological")
    )
  ) 
  # group_by(
  #   result_type, oro_type, result_effect
  # ) %>%
  # summarise(
  #   weightedSum = sum(weight, na.rm = T)
  # )

resultTypes <- levels(tradeOffs$result_type)
oros <- levels(tradeOffs$oro_type)
resultTradeOffs_df <- data.frame()
for(r in 1:length(resultTypes)){
  # for(o in 1:length(oros)){
    posIds <- tradeOffs %>%
      filter(result_type == resultTypes[r], #oro_type == oros[o], 
             result_effect=="Positive")
    posIds <- unique(posIds$id)
    
    dfTemp <- tradeOffs %>%
      filter(id %in% posIds, 
             result_type != resultTypes[r],# oro_type == oros[o], 
             result_effect!="Neutral")
    if(nrow(dfTemp) < 1){
      next
    }
    dfTemp<- dfTemp%>%
      mutate(
        result_effect = factor(result_effect, levels = c("Positive","Negative"))
      ) %>%
      group_by(result_type, result_effect) %>%
      summarise(
        weightedSum = sum(weight, na.rm = T)
      )%>%
      complete(result_effect, fill = list(weightedSum = NA)) %>%
      pivot_wider(
        names_from = result_effect,
        values_from = weightedSum
      ) %>%
      mutate(
        Negative = Negative*-1
      )%>%
      replace_na(list(Positive = 0, Negative = 0))%>%
      mutate(
        # oro_type = oros[o],
        positive_result = resultTypes[r]
      )
    
    resultTradeOffs_df <- resultTradeOffs_df %>%
      bind_rows(dfTemp)
    
  # }
}
resultTradeOffs_df$positive_result <- factor(
  as.character(resultTradeOffs_df$positive_result),
  levels = levels(resultTradeOffs_df$result_type)
)



tradeoff_ggp <- ggplot(resultTradeOffs_df, aes(x=result_type))+
  facet_wrap( ~ positive_result, nrow = 1)+
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult=c(0.15,0.15)))+
  scale_x_discrete(
    limits = rev(levels(resultTradeOffs_df$result_type))
  )+
  geom_rect(aes(x=result_type, width=0.7, ymin = Negative, ymax = 0), fill="#d01c8b")+
  geom_rect(aes(x=result_type, width=0.7, ymin = 0, ymax = Positive), fill="#4dac26")+
  geom_text(data = resultTradeOffs_df %>% filter(0 < Positive),
            aes(y=Positive, label = Positive), hjust=0, size=3)+
  geom_text(data = resultTradeOffs_df %>% filter(Negative < 0),
            aes(y=Negative, label = Negative), hjust=1, size=3)+
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0)+
      labs(
        x = "Adaptation outcome type",
        y = "Adaptive outcomes v. trade-off (weighted sum)"
      )+
      coord_flip(clip = "off")+
      theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
  # theme(
  #   plot.margin = unit(c(rep(0.2,4)), "cm")
  # )
tradeoff_ggp

ggsave(
  filename = here::here("figures/main/coben_disben_barplots.pdf"),
  tradeoff_ggp,
  width = 7, height=4
)



```




```{r trade offs by ORO}

resultTypes <- levels(tradeOffs$result_type)
oros <- levels(tradeOffs$oro_type)
resultTradeOffs_ORO_df <- data.frame()
for(r in 1:length(resultTypes)){
  for(o in 1:length(oros)){
    posIds <- tradeOffs %>%
      filter(result_type == resultTypes[r], oro_type == oros[o], 
             result_effect=="Positive")
    posIds <- unique(posIds$id)
    
    dfTemp <- tradeOffs %>%
      filter(id %in% posIds, 
             result_type != resultTypes[r], oro_type == oros[o], 
             result_effect!="Neutral")
    if(nrow(dfTemp) < 1){
      next
    }
    dfTemp<- dfTemp%>%
      mutate(
        result_effect = factor(result_effect, levels = c("Positive","Negative"))
      ) %>%
      group_by(result_type, result_effect) %>%
      summarise(
        weightedSum = sum(weight, na.rm = T)
      )%>%
      complete(result_effect, fill = list(weightedSum = NA)) %>%
      pivot_wider(
        names_from = result_effect,
        values_from = weightedSum
      ) %>%
      mutate(
        Negative = Negative*-1
      )%>%
      replace_na(list(Positive = 0, Negative = 0))%>%
      mutate(
        oro_type = oros[o],
        positive_result = resultTypes[r]
      )
    
    resultTradeOffs_ORO_df <- resultTradeOffs_ORO_df %>%
      bind_rows(dfTemp)
    
  }
}
resultTradeOffs_ORO_df$oro_type <- factor(
  resultTradeOffs_ORO_df$oro_type,
  levels = oros
)

tradeoff_ORO_ggp <- ggplot(resultTradeOffs_ORO_df, aes(x=result_type))+
  facet_grid(oro_type ~ positive_result, scales = "free_y", labeller = label_wrap_gen(width = 10) )+
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult=c(0.15,0.15)))+
  geom_rect(aes(x=result_type, width=0.7, ymin = Negative, ymax = 0), fill="#d01c8b")+
  geom_rect(aes(x=result_type, width=0.7, ymin = 0, ymax = Positive), fill="#4dac26")+
  geom_text(data = resultTradeOffs_ORO_df %>% filter(0 < Positive),
            aes(y=Positive, label = scales::number(Positive, accuracy = 0.1)), hjust=0, size=2.5)+
  geom_text(data = resultTradeOffs_ORO_df %>% filter(Negative < 0),
            aes(y=Negative, label = scales::number(Negative, accuracy = 0.1)), hjust=1, size=2.5)+
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0)+
      labs(
        x = "Adaptation outcome type",
        y = "Adaptive outcomes v. trade-off (weighted sum)"
      )+
      coord_flip(clip = "off")+
      theme_minimal(base_size = 9)
  # theme(
  #   plot.margin = unit(c(rep(0.2,4)), "cm")
  # )
tradeoff_ORO_ggp

ggsave(
  filename = here::here("figures/main/coben_disben_barplots_by_ORO.pdf"),
  tradeoff_ORO_ggp,
  width = 6, height=7.5
)

```
